rules.title: Never delete production data without verified backup + explicit Owner confirmation
Never deploy to production without a green smoke-test from the same commit
Never overwrite `STRIPE_SECRET_KEY` (or any production secret) on VPS without backing up the .env first
Never write vault files with `cat >> file <<EOF` (shell heredoc)
Never send a Stripe live request (checkout, refund, webhook test) without verifying the active env is `sk_live_` vs `sk_test_`
Any credential obtained via automation (Playwright / OAuth / pasted into a script) NEVER appears in stdout, logs, chat, git, or temp files persisting >5 minutes
Never write a UI component that calls an API before the endpoint resolves (200 or 401)
Never deploy a page whose claims are not backed by shipped infra; on a model change, copy + structured-data + metadata + OG must all agree before cutover
Never hardcode a tenant / brand identifier in a frontend (or any operator-facing) surface; derive it per-session from the backend
Never write a personal datum about a real person without an Owner-confirmed vault entry
rules.trigger:
about to write ANY datum about a real person -- name, profession,
location, age, email, company, job title, or any biographical attribute --
into any file in the repo (page copy, blog posts, JSON-LD, llms.txt, metadata,
OG text, comments, vault). No exception for "obvious" or "public" data.
rules.stop:
run the 3-step gate.
  1. Is this exact datum present in `vault/governance/owner-confirmed-facts.md`
     (or an explicit Owner decision / SL-* entry) as Owner-verified?
  2. If NO -> STOP. Ask the Owner. NEVER invent, infer, or "fill in the
     obvious". A public datum that was invented is still a hallucination.
  3. If YES -> write it, and cite the confirming SL / decision in the change.
The binding legal pages the Owner authored (/terms, /privacy) are an
acceptable confirmation source for identity facts.
rules.evidence:
2026-06-04 -- `/about` shipped "Jacobo López, a Spanish lawyer" (a
hallucination) to production; remediation grep found it across 9 surfaces. Full
inventory: project `vault/governance/personal-data-audit.md`. Owner-only
confirmation file: `vault/governance/owner-confirmed-facts.md`. Detect at the
smoke harness: the rendered `/about` (+ `/es/sobre`) must carry ZERO
profession/biographical claim not in the confirmed-facts file (UT-PII).
rules.exception:
the Owner types an explicit in-session confirmation of the specific
datum (e.g. "yes, you can state <fact>") OR the datum already exists in
`owner-confirmed-facts.md`. "Autonomous mode" does NOT cover inventing PII.

rules.title: Never publish an ad/artifact under a niche label the content does not support; niche-by-scrape-parameter is forbidden
rules.trigger:
any pipeline step that files OR publishes an ad/artifact under a
niche/category label -- ingest tagging, intelligence-post generation, or any
niche-scoped query that feeds a user-facing surface.
rules.stop:
the niche label MUST be content-verified, never
trusted from the producer-side scrape/search parameter. An ad ships under niche N
only if its content scores > 0 for N
(`tuax_core/engines/intelligence/niche_classifier.py`). Done-gate:
`scripts/data_quality_gate.py` purity(N) = verified/total >= 0.90 BEFORE generation
(wired into `deploy/cron-weekly-intelligence.sh` between ingest and generate); the
generator's `filter_relevant` + >=3-relevant hard-fail is the per-post second
layer. Locked by `test_niche_is_not_scrape_parameter`.
rules.evidence:
2026-06-04 -- /blog/2026-w23-fitness-intelligence shipped off-niche ads
under niche=fitness. Root cause niche=scrape-parameter. Full RCA: project
`vault/knowledge_base/SL-58-sprint39-niche-classifier.md`; doctrine
`vault/governance/PROJECT_DOCTRINE.md` §HARD-RULES. Detect at the smoke harness:
the audit `scripts/audit_intelligence_posts.py` over every published
`*-intelligence.mdx` -> 0 contaminated. Data-quality purity is a PRODUCT
requirement, not a nice-to-have: a niche post with < 90% on-niche ads is worse
than NO post -- it damages the system's credibility before the operator. The
data_quality_gate is the final guardian before any publication (UT-DATA-QUALITY).
rules.exception:
Owner types "publish raw scrape niche for <reason>" in-session
(authorizes ONE turn; never for live customer-facing intelligence posts).

rules.title: publish-store is an irreversible real-money action; never execute it without the 3 gates
rules.trigger:
any code path that calls `generate_store_real` (or otherwise creates a
live Shopify product) on behalf of an operator.
rules.stop:
verify ALL THREE, in order, and fail honestly
on the first that does not hold:
  1. an approved `store` supervised_action exists for the tenant
     (`status == approved`) -> else 400, "aprueba el borrador primero";
  2. the operator has an ACTIVE `ShopifyCredential` (keyed by operator EMAIL --
     the identity the OAuth callback + revenue sync store it under, NOT the
     per-session brand slug) -> else 400 + connect CTA to /configuracion;
  3. no `store_live` action already exists for the tenant -> else 409 (never
     duplicate the product).
Any gate failing is a precise 400/409, never a 500 and never a fake success
(Reality Contract). A Shopify rejection surfaces the real userErrors, never the
token (HR-6). Idempotency: the create call carries `publish:<approved_id>` so a
retry de-duplicates at Shopify rather than creating twice.
rules.evidence:
2026-06-05 -- Sprint 42 wired the last cable (approved draft -> live
Shopify product). Identity sub-bug found + fixed at seal: `ShopifyCredential`
is email-keyed while `supervised_actions` are brand-keyed; the Sprint 41 badge
queried by brand and so reported "not connected" for a connected store, which
would have hidden the publish button. Project:
`vault/knowledge_base/SL-63-sprint42-publish-store-live.md`. Detect at the smoke
harness: `POST /api/v1/sge/publish-store` unauth -> 401 (gated), `GET` -> 405
(mounted), never 404 (smoke [100]/[101]).

**Amendment 2026-06-10 (Owner Decision D06) -- gradual publish with operator config.**
The 3 technical gates above are PERMANENT and unchanged. Gate 1 (an approved action)
MAY be satisfied by an operator's STANDING consent -- `user_preferences.auto_approve_publish`
-- but ONLY for a publish Karma classifies as SAFE; a decision that is not clearly safe
ALWAYS asks the operator, even with auto-approve on. Auto-approve is an explicit opt-in
by the operator (default = ask every time), never a system default and never inferred.
This makes publish gradual (Owner choice B over "never autonomous"), without weakening
gates 2-3 or the idempotency. Affects: publish pipeline (`generate_store_real`),
`supervised_actions`, `user_preferences`, `engines/karma/emit.py`. Decision doc
`vault/decisions/owner-decisions-2026-06-10.md` D06. Pairs with HR-KARMA-AUTONOMY
(standing-consent is bounded, never unbounded).
rules.exception:
none. "Autonomous mode" does NOT authorize publishing without the 3
gates -- the gates ARE the authorization.

rules.title: vault benchmark priors and the operator moat are SEPARATE tables; a prior is external knowledge labelled source=vault_prior and NEVER folds into the moat
rules.trigger:
any read/write of niche confidence, niche benchmarks, or the moat feed;
any new "starting intelligence for a niche" surface.
rules.stop:
(1) priors live in their OWN table, never a row in
the moat; (2) the confidence endpoint returns ``source`` explicitly -- ``moat``
(real tests), ``vault_prior`` (external benchmark, no tests yet) or ``none``
(undocumented -> UNKNOWN); (3) the moat ALWAYS wins once it has >=1 real test
for the niche -- a prior only fills the cold-start gap; (4) the operator always
sees the source label, so an external benchmark is never presented as operator
evidence. A prior is a hypothesis the operator's real test replaces, not a fact.
rules.evidence:
2026-06-05 -- Sprint 46b added vault niche priors so fitness reads MEDIUM
from day 1 instead of UNKNOWN. Owner decision 1 sealed "priors en tabla separada,
el moat queda puro/vacio". Implementation: ``niche_benchmark_priors`` (table) +
``niche_priors_repo.get_prior`` (table -> YAML fallback) + ``fwe_router``
``_resolve_confidence`` (moat-first, prior-fallback, source-labelled). Detect:
``test_sprint46b_collective_intelligence.py`` asserts source=vault_prior on an
empty moat and source=moat after one real outcome; the moat model has no operator
column. Project: ``vault/knowledge_base/SL-68-sprint46b-collective-intelligence.md``.
rules.exception:
none. "Autonomous mode" does not authorize seeding the moat from
priors -- the separation IS the integrity guarantee.

rules.title: no first test launches as a paid run without passing the pre-market validation gate (5 checks); losing capital on a niche with no demand evidence is a SYSTEM failure, not an operator failure
rules.trigger:
any path that launches / approves a paid test (confirm_launch_test, any
future per-operator ad executor), or any "should I test this niche" surface.
rules.stop:
(1) run the 5-check gate
(``engines/first_win/pre_market_gate.evaluate_pre_market_gate``) BEFORE creating
the test plan; (2) KILL (economics impossible even at best-case CPA) and
NEEDS_REWORK (not demonstrable / saturated auction / over the band cap) BLOCK the
launch with a 409 + the failed checks; (3) no paid demand this week degrades to
APPROVED_ORGANIC_ONLY (validate organic first), never a blind paid launch; (4)
the gate's demand + saturation inputs are the REAL KobiiSpy ad_performance_signals
aggregate, never a fabricated competitor count.
rules.evidence:
2026-06-06 -- Sprint 47 built the unified validation panel + the
pre-market gate. WDE was found to be per-CANDIDATE (TEST/WATCH/REJECT), not
per-niche, so niche saturation/competition derive from ad_performance_signals
counts (the table has no advertiser column; "competitors" = distinct active ads,
never invented). Detect:
``tests/api/test_sprint47_validation_intelligence.py`` shows each blocking check
blocks and no-demand -> organic-only. Project:
``vault/knowledge_base/SL-69-sprint47-validation-intelligence.md``.
rules.exception:
none. "Autonomous mode" does not authorize bypassing the gate -- the
gate IS the capital-protection guarantee.

rules.title: no synchronous operator request makes more than ONE LLM call; LLM cost grows linearly with operators, so an un-cached / mis-routed call path is a SYSTEM cost-defect, not a runtime detail
rules.trigger:
any request handler that would call an LLM more than once, or any new
LLM-calling code path.
rules.stop:
a synchronous operator request may make
AT MOST ONE LLM call. If multiple outputs are needed: (1) use a deterministic
template where possible (the operator creative path is already template-only, no
LLM); (2) combine into a single LLM call; or (3) move it to a Celery worker
(the CIE batch path already does -- ingest returns 202 + apply_async). Cacheable
deterministic-input generations MUST go through ``engines/llm_cache.cached_or_generate``
and MUST be model-routed via ``engines/llm_routing.route_model`` (cheap model for
bulk creative / classification / screening; full model only for money decisions).
rules.evidence:
2026-06-06 -- Sprint 48 audited every LLM call (5 sites), found the
generation adapters hardcoded Sonnet with no routing and the MIE cache was an
ephemeral in-process dict. Fix = durable ``llm_response_cache`` + task->model
router + a guard test that the batch path stays async. Detect:
``tests/api/test_sprint48_cost_optimization.py`` (route concept->cheap,
kill_scale->full; cache miss-then-hit; cie ingest dispatches apply_async).
Project: ``vault/knowledge_base/SL-70-sprint48-cost-optimization.md``.
rules.exception:
the interactive chats (coo / assistant) make one bounded LLM call per
turn by design (Haiku + Anthropic prompt-cache + turn cap) -- that is the single
allowed call, not a fan-out.

rules.title: an ad NEVER launches on the Owner/system account for an operator's test; the launcher ALWAYS reads the tenant's OWN credential + OWN ad account
rules.trigger:
any code path that launches, pauses, or reads spend for an ad on behalf
of a tenant (the launcher, the spend puller, the auto-kill).
rules.stop:
the credential vault MUST be tenant-scoped -- the repo derives the
tenant from ``session.info['brand_id']`` (set by ``UnitOfWork(brand_id=...)``) and
filters every read. The launcher reads the operator's ``meta_ad_account_id``
(``OperatorProfile``) + decrypts THEIR credential, and records the
``ad_executions.ad_account_id`` it ran in (audit proof). ``brand_id="default"`` /
``Settings().meta_ads_account_id`` in an EXECUTION path = Hard Stop. An operator
missing an account/cred is SKIPPED, never run with the system token. HR-6 still
applies: the decrypted token rides in the Authorization header, never the URL.
rules.evidence:
2026-06-07 -- Fase-0 found ``api_credentials`` had no tenant column and
``find_latest_by_platform`` returned the GLOBALLY-latest row, so a 2nd operator's
test would fire on the Owner's account. Fix = ``api_credentials.tenant_id`` +
tenant-scoped repo + the per-operator launcher/spend-puller/kill. Detect:
``test_api_credential_tenant_isolation`` + ``test_sprint49_b2_executor`` (operator
token + ``act_<their id>`` reach the mock). Project: SL-71.

rules.title: spend caps are applied PER operator from their capital_band; a global cap shared by all operators violates FWE capital protection
rules.trigger: any pre-spend authorization for an operator's ad.
rules.stop:
``capital_guard.authorize_spend`` runs with the operator's
``FirstWinProfile.capital_band``; the euro ceilings come ONLY from
``capital_caps.CAPS`` (observe 0 / validation 10·30 / micro_test 25·75 /
first_scale 50·150). ``already_spent_today`` accrues from the operator's own
``ad_executions`` so a 2nd same-day launch is blocked once the daily cap is
committed. A blocked launch records NOTHING on the platform.
rules.evidence:
2026-06-07 -- the per-band caps + guard already existed (Sprint 44b) but
nothing read the OPERATOR's band at execution time. Fix = wire ``authorize_spend``
into the launcher with the tenant's band. Detect:
``test_sprint49_b4_iron_vault`` (validation band blocks over-daily budget +
accumulation). Project: SL-71.

rules.title: CPA/ROAS from temporal order-overlap is an ESTIMATE, never presented as exact pixel attribution
rules.trigger:
any surface that shows a CPA / ROAS / conversion figure derived from
order-window overlap (the dashboard, an email, an API response, a kill reason).
rules.stop:
every such surface MUST label the figure as "estimado" and a
value that cannot be computed (no conversions yet / no spend yet / store
unreadable) renders as "--" or None, NEVER 0 (UT-58 family: None != 0 -- a zero
would falsely read as "no demand" / "infinitely cheap" and could trip a kill).
The conversion-metrics layer stores ``conversions=None`` when the Shopify read
is unavailable, so a transient outage is never recorded as a real zero.
rules.evidence:
2026-06-07 -- Sprint 50 built the conversion feed (Shopify orders since
``launched_at``) that the auto-kill needs. The honest framing is mandatory
because the number drives real spend decisions. Detect:
``test_sprint50_b1_conversion`` (compute_metrics None discipline) +
``test_sprint50_b1_refresh`` (no-store -> conversions None). Project: SL-72.

rules.title: the moat persists context_slices, not only aggregate statistics; aggregating an outcome without its slice is forbidden
rules.trigger:
any code that records / folds a completed test outcome, or reads "what
works" for a niche.
rules.stop:
outcomes that carry their full slice
(mode + platform + hook_type + awareness_level) MUST be folded into
``causal_performance_memory`` (the CPM) IN ADDITION to the niche aggregate -- the
record_outcome path does both. The CPM is a SEPARATE additive table (Owner
decision 2026-06-07), NEVER columns bolted onto ``niche_intelligence_moat`` -- the
moat's anonymity-by-schema (HR-COLLECTIVE-DATA: no tenant column, one row/niche)
stays intact. Like the moat, the CPM has NO operator column. With context_slices +
the inheritance hierarchy (A3), the moat is incopiable; without them it is a
public-knowledge average.
rules.evidence:
2026-06-07 -- Fase-0 found ``niche_intelligence_moat`` was aggregate-only
and incompatible with slices. Fix = additive ``causal_performance_memory`` +
``causal_memory_repo.record_slice`` + record_outcome fold + niche-confidence
top_causal_hook. Detect: ``test_causal_memory_repo`` (record_outcome folds CPM;
top hook by ego_score). Project: SL-76.
rules.exception:
an outcome with no slice context (legacy / manual self-report missing
the dims) folds into the niche aggregate ONLY -- that is allowed (backward compat),
not a violation. The rule forbids DISCARDING a slice that IS present, not requiring
one that is absent.

rules.title: EGO_ADS != EGO_ORGANIC; the two objective functions are distinct, versioned, and Owner-gated to change
rules.trigger:
any creative scoring / ranking, any change to the EGO weights, any new
mode-bearing generation path.
rules.stop:
the objective function is mode-specific and explicit
(``engines/cie/ego.py``): EGO_ADS = hook 0.45 / claim_risk 0.35 / cost 0.20
(blocks claim_risk HIGH); EGO_ORGANIC = hook 0.60 / claim_risk 0.30 / cost 0.10
(STRICTER -- blocks claim_risk MEDIUM+, no ad-review buffer). Inheritance + CPM
reads NEVER cross mode. Changing any weight requires Owner approval + a version
bump (the weights are a constant table, not a tuned hyperparameter touched
silently).
rules.evidence:
2026-06-07 -- A2 made the objective explicit per mode + added
claim_risk_by_category to hooks_library.yaml. Detect:
``test_ego`` (high blocked in ads; medium blocked in organic not ads; same input
-> distinct score per mode). Project: SL-76.
rules.exception:
none for collapsing the two into one. Owner may re-tune a specific
weight in-session with an explicit instruction + version bump.

rules.title: no store is declared "ready to publish" without passing the auditor; the auditor never BLOCKS the publish (the operator decides) but its verdict is always visible + recorded
rules.trigger:
any surface that presents a generated store as ready to approve/publish
(Guided Start step 3, the publish-store path, the dashboard store badge).
rules.stop:
the store audit (POST /api/v1/store/audit, 8 dimensions ->
GO/FIX/NOGO/KILL) runs and its verdict is shown BEFORE the operator approves,
and the verdict is recorded. The auditor does NOT block the publish -- the
operator decides -- but a publish with a NOGO verdict happens with COMPLETE
information (the specific reasons + the fixes), never blind. GO requires D1
(product) + D2 (offer) + D6 (ads-readiness) + D7 (capital) PASS; a FAIL in D1 /
D6 / D7 is an automatic NOGO (no demand / no ad capability / no capital).
rules.evidence:
2026-06-07 -- the "Tienda de Shopify Autonoma" doc flagged the missing
auditor as the most critical autonomy gap. Detect: smoke [138]-[141]
(/store/audit + /store/audit/fix mounted + gated); ``test_store_auditor`` +
``test_store_audit_router`` (verdict logic + 8 dims + no-capital NOGO). Project:
SL-78. Pairs with UT-AUDIT-NOGO-REASON + HR-PUBLISH.
rules.exception:
none. "Autonomous mode" does not authorize hiding the audit -- the
visibility IS the guarantee. (Distinct from HR-PUBLISH, which gates the
irreversible Shopify action; HR-AUDIT-BEFORE-PUBLISH governs the pre-publish
judgement that informs it.)

rules.title: a store generated by the SGE never reaches the operator with a content score < 70/100; the score REUSES the SL-78 auditor's content dimensions (D2-D5), never an invented scorer
rules.trigger:
any surface that presents an SGE-generated store draft to the operator
(Guided Start step 3 draft, the dashboard, the publish path).
rules.stop:
the draft is scored by quality_gate.score_store_content, which REUSES
store_auditor._d2_offer/_d3_content/_d4_page/_d5_trust + _score -- NO duplicate
scorer, so the two surfaces never drift. Below 70 the system auto-iterates the
existing auto_fix (D4 FAQ schema / D2 niche guarantee) up to 3x and re-scores. If
it still cannot reach 70 for a niche, report to the Owner -- never present a
mediocre store. The score is the auditor's real dimension logic, never invented
criteria. The gate INFORMS, it does not hard-block (mirrors HR-AUDIT-BEFORE-PUBLISH).
rules.evidence:
2026-06-08 -- SGE v2 turned the skeleton generator into a professional
store builder. Detect: smoke [142] (/sge/generate-onboarding mounted + gated);
test_sge_v2_store_builder (quality score >= floor, ensure_quality iteration,
honest image WARN). Project: SL-79. Pairs with UT-COPY-AWARENESS-MAPPED +
HR-AUDIT-BEFORE-PUBLISH.
rules.exception:
none. The honest "image pending" WARN (D3, no image pipeline yet) is
kept as a WARN, never faked to a PASS to clear the floor.

rules.title: the Product Research Engine recommends ONLY products with verified demand evidence (a vault-documented winner or a live KobiiSpy signal); it never invents a candidate
rules.trigger:
any surface that recommends a product to pursue (GET research products,
Guided Start Paso 1, the reform add-product).
rules.stop:
every candidate comes from niche_products.yaml (a documented section-2
winner) and the live ad_performance_signals reading (KobiiSpy). KobiiSpy carries
product_category + trend + counts but NO product_name, so named candidates are
vault-sourced and KobiiSpy corroborates demand, competition and trend. A niche
with neither a documented candidate nor a live signal returns an honest GAP
report, never a fabricated product.
rules.evidence:
2026-06-08 -- the system could build a professional store but could not
say WHAT to sell. Detect: smoke [143] (research products mounted + gated);
test_product_research (ranking, gap, no-invention) + test_research_router (READY
candidates + GAP). Project: SL-80. Pairs with UT-PRODUCT-FIT-SCORE.
rules.exception: none. Autonomous mode does not authorize inventing a winner.

rules.title: before modifying ANY element of an operator's existing store, the system requires a supervised_action approved explicitly by that operator; reform PROPOSES, it never touches the live store on its own
rules.trigger: POST store reform and any future apply-reform path.
rules.stop:
the reform endpoint is READ-ONLY -- it lists existing products
(get_products), classifies KEEP vs REFORM from the live product body, proposes
the new researched product (SGE v2 draft), and persists a PENDING
supervised_action carrying the plan. It performs ZERO Shopify writes. Applying
the plan is a separate step gated on the operator approving that action; the
Shopify backup is verified before any change executes.
rules.evidence:
2026-06-08 -- Alvaro (pilot operator 2) has an existing store to reform
(vault clients alvaro-backlog FASE 3). Detect: smoke [144]+[145] (reform mounted
+ gated + POST-only); test_research_router (reform 400 without Shopify, classify
keeps vs reforms, never deletes). Project: SL-80. Pairs with HR-PUBLISH +
HR-PRODUCT-EVIDENCE.
rules.exception: none.

rules.title: access to /pricing (and /es/precios) requires a valid invitation token OR an active session; a visitor with neither sees the waitlist, never the prices; the gate fails CLOSED
rules.trigger:
any request to /pricing or /es/precios; any change to the access-gate
or the invitation resolver.
rules.stop:
the pricing pages run evaluatePricingGate server-side (force-dynamic):
active cw_session OR a valid invitation_token in the URL opens the plans; no
token + no session -> redirect to /waitlist; expired/used token -> honest
"request a new invitation" page. The gate FAILS CLOSED -- if the invitation
resolver is unreachable the token is treated as invalid and the visitor is
bounced; the plans are NEVER silently granted on an infra hiccup. Invitation
tokens are bound to one email, single-use, 7-day expiry.
rules.evidence:
2026-06-08 -- the public surface was open before launch. Detect: smoke
[148] (bare /pricing bounces to /waitlist) + the token-bearing /pricing content
checks (seeded smoke invitation) + test_waitlist_router (invite/resolve
transitions). Project: SL-81. Pairs with UT-INVITATION-IDEMPOTENT.
rules.exception: an authenticated account (active session) always sees the plans.

rules.title: a generated AEO fast_answer must be a specific, actionable sentence (real count + platform breakdown + dominant hook + where to act); a generic "concentrated in N platforms" filler is forbidden
rules.trigger:
any code path that emits a `fast_answer` frontmatter field or an on-page
"Fast answer" / "Respuesta rapida" block (the intelligence-post generator, any
future AEO content emitter).
rules.stop:
the fast_answer MUST carry concrete signal -- a
real count, the per-platform breakdown (e.g. "meta (4), tiktok (1)"), the
dominant hook type, and where to act -- assembled from THIS post's real data,
never a templated "concentrated in N platforms" string. It is one sentence,
specific, and grammatical in both locales. A value that cannot be made specific
(no data) means the post hard-fails and writes nothing (Delta 11 / HR-NICHE-01),
never a padded generic line.
rules.evidence:
2026-06-08 -- the weekly intelligence generator emitted
`fast_answer: "In fitness, this week's top 5 ad signals concentrated in 1
platforms. The full ranking is below."` -- generic, ungrammatical ("1 platforms"),
zero actionable signal. Part A rewrote `write_post` to build the fast_answer from
a real platform Counter + dominant hook. Detect: the audit asserts no published
`*-intelligence.mdx` carries the "concentrated in" filler. Project: SL-84. Pairs
with UT-NO-MARKDOWN-LEAKS.
rules.exception: none. "Autonomous mode" does not authorize shipping generic AEO filler.

rules.title: Karma's per-event autonomy always starts at OBSERVE and is EARNED through real confirmations, never assigned; a capital-committing ACT is additionally gated by the operator's global earned AutonomyTier (the capital ceiling)
rules.trigger:
any Karma event surfaced through engines/karma/emit.emit_karma_event, any
change to karma_preferences autonomy levels, any new Karma sub-agent.
rules.stop:
every new (operator, event_type) starts at OBSERVE. Autonomy is
earned ONLY by real confirmations (3 consecutive -> RECOMMEND, 3 more -> ACT); 2
consecutive dismissals demote / silence. A per-event ACT may auto-handle NON-capital
events freely, but an ACT whose action commits real capital (ad_killed -> relaunch =
fresh spend, ActionCode.SCALE_ADS) ALSO requires the global earned AutonomyTier to
permit that ActionCode -- below it Karma falls back to notify-only and NEVER records
"acted" for an action it did not perform. No operator is granted ACT by default.
rules.evidence:
2026-06-08 -- SL-85 built Karma on the existing 6-tier AutonomyTier +
supervised_actions substrate (no new autonomy model). Detect:
tuax_core/tests/engines/karma/test_emit (capital ceiling blocks ACT below tier;
runner not executed) + test_preferences (OBSERVE default, 3-confirm ladder,
2-dismiss demote). Pairs with HR-KARMA-IDENTITY + UT-KARMA-SPECIFIC-MESSAGES.
rules.exception:
the operator manually sets a level in /app/configuracion -- that is the
operator's own informed choice, not an assignment by the system.

rules.title: Karma is ONE entity to the operator; the internal sub-agents (Screen Agent, Kill Alert, Market Pulse, ...) are never surfaced as technical names
rules.trigger:
any operator-facing Karma surface -- a karma_message, a Web Push, a panel
label, the preferences UI, a backlog agent that will ship into Karma.
rules.stop:
the operator only ever sees "Karma". Internal agent names stay in
code and docs; messages, the /app/karma panel, the configuracion preferences, and
the push notification all speak as one entity. A new sub-agent plugs into the
karma_events bus + the existing per-event autonomy; it never gets its own
operator-facing brand.
rules.evidence:
2026-06-08 -- SL-85 shipped 5 event types from distinct sources (kill,

rules.title: any feature with a verifiable done-gate and NO human decision between iterations MUST be built as a self-iterating loop (execute -> verify the gate -> generate the corrective next step -> iterate, max 3, then STOP+report); NEVER applied to economic or irreversible actions
rules.trigger:
building or extending any generator whose output is validated by a
deterministic done-gate that needs no human judgement between attempts -- SGE
quality_gate (store score >= 70), blog fast_answer (<= 60 words + a real datum),
Andromeda creative batch (diversity >= 4 levels), and the like.
rules.stop:
implement it as a bounded
self-prompting loop -- generate -> run the done-gate -> if it fails, feed the
gate's specific failure back in as context and regenerate -> iterate until the
gate passes OR a hard cap of 3 attempts is reached; on exhaustion STOP and report
the last failure honestly (never ship the failing artifact, never claim success).
To activate the pattern inside a Claude Code prompt, append to the done-gate:
"Si este done-gate no pasa: genera el siguiente prompt correctivo y ejecutalo.
Maximo 3 iteraciones. Si tras 3 sigue sin pasar: STOP y reportar."
rules.evidence:
2026-06-08 -- SL-86 shipped the Revenue Scale Calculator (a deterministic,
gate-checkable projector) and observed that the existing gate-checked generators
(SGE quality_gate ensure_quality, blog no-markdown-leak, Andromeda batch) already
embody this loop; codifying it makes future gate-checked features inherit the
iterate-to-green discipline by default instead of one-shotting. Pairs with the
2-CONSECUTIVE-FAILURES LAW (a loop converges or STOPS, it never thrashes).
rules.exception:
NEVER auto-iterate an economic decision (kill / scale / spend) or any
irreversible action -- those keep their human or earned-autonomy gate (HR-KARMA-
AUTONOMY, HR-FWE-CAPITAL). The loop is for content/quality generation only.

rules.title: a WINNER in ProfitState is the START of the maturity process, not the end; profit is not reinvestable/withdrawable until the refund window has matured + the reserve is constituted, and reinvestable is ALWAYS net (verified_profit - refund_reserve), never gross
rules.trigger:
any path that scales/reinvests on profit, or presents profit as available
to reinvest/withdraw (the CAE scale pipeline, the Revenue Scale Calculator, any
"how much can I reinvest/withdraw" surface).
rules.stop:
(1) profit placed on the maturity ladder
(``engines/aio/profit_maturity.evaluate_maturity``): UNVERIFIED -> PENDING ->
[AT_RISK] -> VERIFIED -> PROTECTED -> REINVESTABLE -> WITHDRAWABLE, driven by
elapsed time since the sale + refund_rate + reserve_constituted; (2) the
false-profit gate (``false_profit_gate.scale_gate``) BLOCKS scaling until
REINVESTABLE with a specific, time-bounded message; withdraw_gate WARNS (never
blocks -- the operator's money) until WITHDRAWABLE; (3) reinvestable is ALWAYS net:
``reserve_breakdown`` returns reinvestable = verified_profit - (gross_revenue *
refund_rate), surfaced to the operator, NEVER the gross figure. The CAE maturity
enforcement is OPT-IN (default off) so it never silently changes live scaling --
turning it on is an Owner decision, but when on the block is real.
rules.evidence:
2026-06-09 -- SL-88 built profit_maturity + refund_reserve + false_profit_gate
and wired the gate into the CAE scale pipeline + the scale calculator. Detect at the
smoke harness: [173] young->UNVERIFIED + aged+reserve->REINVESTABLE; [174] immature
profit blocks scaling with a time-bounded message; [175] reinvestable is net not
gross. Project: ``vault/knowledge_base/SL-88-sprint-profit-maturity-savings.md``.
Pairs with HR-SAVINGS-LEDGER + HR-VALIDATION-GATE.
rules.exception:
NEVER auto-iterate the scale/withdraw decision (HR-SELF-PROMPTING-LOOP
excludes economic/irreversible actions). The gate informs; the operator/earned
autonomy decides.

rules.title: a saving is recorded ONLY at a discrete avoided-cost moment (an automatic kill, a pre-market block), idempotent on ref_id; NEVER on a polled read, and a zero period is an honest zero, never a fabricated figure
rules.trigger:

rules.stop:
(1) a SavingsEvent (``engines/economics/cost_objects``)
is written to ``savings_ledger`` ONLY at a DISCRETE avoided-cost moment -- an
automatic kill (unspent budget protected), a pre-market gate block (avoided test
budget) -- NEVER on a GET / polled read; (2) the write is idempotent on ``ref_id``
(execution id for a kill; niche:verdict:date for a block) so a retry cannot
double-count; (3) the recording is best-effort and NEVER breaks the underlying
action (a ledger failure never un-kills an ad or changes a gate verdict); (4) a
period with no savings returns total 0 + the honest "Todo en orden" text, NEVER a
fabricated figure (Reality Contract).
rules.evidence:

/api/v1/karma/savings) and wired 3 discrete sources (kill_executor unspent budget,

[176] savings_ledger registered in cw_init_db._CW_TABLES; [177] GET /karma/savings
mounted + gated (401). Project: ``vault/knowledge_base/SL-88-sprint-profit-maturity-savings.md``.
Pairs with HR-PROFIT-MATURITY + HR-KARMA-IDENTITY.
rules.exception:
none. "Autonomous mode" does not authorize recording a saving on a poll
or fabricating a non-zero figure -- the discrete-moment rule IS the integrity guarantee.

### UT-SCALE-EXECUTOR-MIRROR -- the scale_executor follows the SAME structure as the kill_executor; kill and scale are symmetric opposites of one economic-decision system, so a structural divergence between them is a bug, not a feature
Sealed 2026-06-10 (SL-90, TUA-X, P1 #4). The system that kills losers and the
system that scales winners are two halves of one autonomy loop. Built as mirrors,
they share the same correctness proofs: pure decision -> authority/guard -> act on
the operator's own token (HR-6) -> surface through Karma.

RECOGNISE: a scale path that invents its own decision shape, its own credential
handling, or its own Karma seam instead of mirroring the kill path. auto_scale
mirrors auto_kill (pure ScaleRuntimeState -> ScaleDecision); scale_executor mirrors
kill_executor (load FWP -> decision -> operator-token action -> emit -> persist);
both run from the same ad_spend_pull tick (kill pass, then scale pass).

Detect: test_auto_scale + test_scale_executor (decision symmetry, OBSERVE event,
ACT live budget, Iron Vault clamp) + smoke scale_decision_fires [183]. Project:
SL-90. Pairs with HR-SCALE-VERIFIED-PROFIT + HR-SCALE-IRON-VAULT.
rules.exception: none -- the cap is raised only by an Owner-confirmed edit to capital_caps.

### UT-DASH-NOT-ZERO -- numeric money/perf fields for a day-1 / no-activity operator show '--' (dash), never '0'; '0' implies the operator operated and earned nothing, '--' implies no data yet -- distinct states with distinct weight
Sealed 2026-06-10 (SL-91, TUA-X). The system communicates with precision: a
brand-new operator who sees '0 revenue' reads failure; one who sees '--' reads
'not started yet'. The two are different truths and must render differently.

RECOGNISE: a dashboard numeric (revenue, spend, ROAS, profit) that coalesces a
missing value to 0. The aggregator returns None for an unmeasured money/perf field
and the UI renders None as '--'; a real measured 0 may show 0, but absence never
does. Counts (active ads) may show a real 0 / 'Sin ads activos' -- the rule governs
money/perf magnitudes, not counts.

Detect: test_command_center (_pulse_metrics None-not-0) + smoke
command_center_pulse_day1 [190] + OperatorCommandCenter eur()/roas() '--' helpers.
Project: SL-91. Pairs with HR-COMMAND-CENTER + HR-ATTRIBUTION-HONEST.

rules.title: the system periodically verifies that every OPEN economic loop is consistent; an unswept open loop is a silent capital risk, not an acceptable steady state
rules.trigger:
any feature that opens an economic loop (commits spend, records revenue,
changes a live budget) without an in-band, immediate confirmation that it closed.
rules.stop:
the loop MUST be reconcilable -- there must be a detectable signal (a
freshness timestamp, a computed-economics field, a confirmed-budget read) the periodic
reconciler can check, and an open loop MUST surface as an informative
reconciliation_alert (never silently accumulate). A detector fires only on a real,
observable inconsistency; it never invents a loop from absent data, and where a state
is pure/computed (e.g. ProfitMaturity) it surfaces the real inconsistency, never a
phantom DB write (Reality Contract).
rules.evidence:
2026-06-10 -- SL-92 engines/economics/economic_reconciliation.py (3 detectors:
ad_no_spend / revenue_no_economics / scale_no_confirm) + economics.reconcile beat (4h).
Project: SL-92. Pairs with UT-RECONCILIATION-SAVES + HR-ATTRIBUTION-HONEST.

### UT-RECONCILIATION-SAVES -- a reconciler that detects + resolves a loop which avoids a real cost MUST record a SavingsEvent; reconciliation is demonstrable economic value, not just technical correctness
Sealed 2026-06-10 (SL-92, TUA-X). If the reconciler only corrected state silently, its
value could neither be measured nor shown to the operator. When closing a loop avoids a
real cost, it books the avoided cost into the Savings Ledger so the operator sees what
the integrity layer protected.

RECOGNISE: a reconciler/guard that resolves an avoidable cost but writes nothing to the
Savings Ledger. The honest amount is the real capital-at-risk (e.g. the daily budget of
an ad brought back under monitoring), confidence LOW for an avoided-RISK estimate --
never inflated; loop types with no honest euro to claim book nothing (no fabrication).
Idempotent on ref_id so a re-sweep never double-counts.

Detect: test_economic_reconciliation (sink books source='reconciliation' + idempotent)
+ smoke reconciliation_savings_booked [197]. Project: SL-92. Pairs with
HR-ECONOMIC-RECONCILIATION + HR-SAVINGS-LEDGER.
rules.exception: none -- a loop with no reconciliation signal is incomplete by definition.

rules.title: every consumption of a costly resource is recorded in usage_events; a resource consumed without a record is an invisible cost that scales indefinitely with the user count
rules.trigger:
any new costly resource (an LLM call, a render, a research sweep, an audit,
an external-API batch) reachable by an operator.
rules.stop:
the resource MUST record a UsageEvent AFTER it succeeds (never meter a
resource that was not consumed; never meter a request blocked by a cap). cost_usd is
an honest ESTIMATE -- None when not estimable, never a fabricated 0 (UT-DASH-NOT-ZERO).
A plan-gated feature additionally needs a soft cap: warn the operator at 80% and, at
100%, refuse with a recoverable error that names the feature and the exact reset date
(never a silent overage, never a hard wall without a way back).
rules.evidence:

rules.title: a taste sub-check threshold differs by niche ONLY when the draft genuinely contains the measured element, and only by changing the MEASUREMENT, never by lowering a bar to pass thin content
rules.trigger: any change to a quality_gate taste sub-check (trust_contrast / faq_depth / value_specificity / anti_slop) threshold or logic that is motivated by a specific niche WARNing.
rules.stop: a niche-specific relaxation is permitted ONLY when (1) the draft genuinely CONTAINS the element the check measures, and the calibration changes only the MEASUREMENT -- it never lowers a bar to pass content that is actually thin; AND (2) the relaxation is scoped to that niche by a draft-internal, niche-derived signal (e.g. is_unique / VisualArtwork JSON-LD) so every other niche stays byte-identical (no regression). For arte concretely: trust_contrast is satisfied by the signed authenticity certificate + return guarantee (art's trust vehicle is NOT review-style social proof); faq_depth is met by ENRICHING the FAQ answers to genuinely >=25 words, the 25-word threshold UNCHANGED. FORBIDDEN: lowering a threshold so a thin draft passes -- that is gaming, equivalent to fabricating the missing element. If the draft does NOT contain the element, the correct fix is to GENERATE the element, never to drop the threshold.

Metric: arte taste WARN->PASS, 75->80; fitness/supplements byte-unchanged; 0 regressions. Pairs with HR-TASTE-DETERMINISTIC + HR-NO-FABRICATED-COPY.

rules.title: A surface is not "visually done" until its design system is canonical, centrally propagated, written, enforced, pixel-validated — and verified against the PROD branch, not a stale checkout
rules.trigger:
** declaring any UI surface visually finished, OR diagnosing a design rule as
"missing", "not applied", or "never written".

**ACCIÓN — STOP. A surface is not visually done if any of these hold:**
1. A UI element (button, badge, callout) has no explicit, documented canonical design system.
2. The design system was applied page-by-page by hand instead of through a central
   class/component that propagates automatically.
3. A design Hard Rule is referenced in conversation but never written into the repository —
   an unwritten rule is an intention without enforcement, not a rule.
4. No grep-gate or lint-rule blocks the non-conforming pattern in pre-commit / CI.

**Mandatory process for any new design decision:**
- a) Define the exact token/value — the hex, the radius, the inset — never "a nice blue".
- b) Apply it in the central class/component once.
- c) Verify it propagates automatically (grep for zero violations).
- d) Create a component wrapper so new code inherits it without remembering the CSS class.
- e) Add the enforcement (lint-rule or grep-gate in CI) that blocks a raw `<button>`/`<div>`
     outside the system.
- f) Document the Hard Rule in the repo — never only in memory or a prior conversation.
- g) Human pixel-validation before declaring "visually verified" — a design hypothesis is not
     a fact until a human confirms it on a real render (HR-VISUAL-01 precedent).
- h) Verify the real state against the canonical PROD branch BEFORE diagnosing "missing" or
     "not written" — `git fetch` + diff against `origin/main` (or the project's prod branch),
     never from the local checkout.

about to declare "X does not exist", "we need to build X from scratch",
"the system has no Y", or to scope a sprint as net-new construction.

about to declare a dataset's content "understood", to summarize it, or to

## §UKDL-TRAPS (Recognized patterns — apply before next attempt)

### UT-1 (cross-ref U-14) — PostgreSQL CHECK constraints with booleans require explicit cast
`is_aspirational = true` is a PG type-error; use `(is_aspirational::text = 'true')`
or `is_aspirational IS TRUE`. SQLAlchemy CHECK constraints in `__table_args__`
must be ANSI-portable so the SQLite test tier doesn't crash.

### UT-2 (cross-ref U-13b) — Windows: NEVER run `python`/`pytest`/`pip`/`uv` directly via Bash
The MSYS2 transport drops stdout silently on verbose Python output. Use the
PowerShell tool with the absolute Python path:
`& 'C:\Users\User\AppData\Local\Programs\Python\Python312\python.exe' -m pytest ...`
Cross-repo doctrine. Sealed 2026-05-28.

### UT-3 — `starlette` version pin required: `<0.39` with `fastapi 0.115`
Newer starlette changed TestClient cookie semantics (per-request `cookies=` is
deprecated; pin to client instance). Bumping starlette without bumping fastapi
breaks ~30+ existing test files cross-project. Verify with `pip show starlette`.

### UT-9 — Next.js 16 colocated `opengraph-image.tsx` + `generateStaticParams` + `generateImageMetadata` collide
Sprint 12 M7 (TUA-X) shipped `app/blog/[slug]/opengraph-image.tsx` for per-post OG images. First push failed `npm run build` with `Cannot find module for page: /es/blog/[slug]/opengraph-image` (ENOENT) during the "Collecting page data" pass.

Root cause: the colocated OG file had BOTH `generateStaticParams` AND `generateImageMetadata` exports while the parent `[slug]/page.tsx` already provided `generateStaticParams`. Next 16 fails page-data resolution when child-route static params duplicate the parent's, and `generateImageMetadata` returning a per-id entry compounds the conflict.

Fix: in the colocated `opengraph-image.tsx`, remove `generateStaticParams` AND `generateImageMetadata`. Keep only:
- `export const size = { width: 1200, height: 630 }`
- `export const contentType = "image/png"`
- `export default async function Image({ params }: { params: Promise<Params> }) { ... }`

The parent route's `generateStaticParams` propagates `params` automatically. Each blog slug still gets its own per-post OG image; Next bakes one per slug at build time.

### UT-10 — Next.js 16 `metadataBase` defaults to `http://localhost:3000` on prod when not set
Sprint 13 T5 (TUA-X) discovered every page's `og:image` URL on the live deploy was `http://localhost:3000/<path>/opengraph-image?<hash>` — pointing at localhost instead of the production host. WhatsApp / Slack / Twitter share-previews failed silently because the image URL was unreachable.

Root cause: `frontend/app/layout.tsx` had no `metadataBase` field on its `Metadata` export. Per Next 16 docs, omitted `metadataBase` defaults to `http://localhost:3000` even in production builds, and resolution of relative URLs in any nested route's metadata uses that base.

Fix: in the root layout, set `metadataBase: new URL(SITE_URL)` where `SITE_URL` is the single source of truth (Sprint 10 M1: `frontend/lib/seo/site-metadata.ts`). One line. Everything downstream resolves correctly.

Detect this at the smoke harness: grep the rendered HTML for `<meta property="og:image" content="http://localhost:3000`. Catch should be added to smoke as Sprint 13 T6 deliverable.

### UT-15 — Signed CDN URLs from third-party libraries embed an expiry; never store and reuse > 1 hour
Sprint 17 T3 / Sprint 16 video probe (TUA-X / kobiispy) — the TikTok Ad Library detail page's `<video>` element exposes a real CDN URL of the shape `https://library.tiktok.com/api/v1/cdn/<id>/video/<b64>/<uuid>?...&signature=<base64>&l=<YYYYMMDDHHMMSS>&...`. The `l=` parameter encodes the signature expiry timestamp, typically ~9 hours past probe time. Storing the URL in a DB / queue / cache and reusing it days later returns 403 / 404 with no clear error.

Root cause: TikTok's CDN signs URLs to prevent hot-linking. Same pattern applies to Meta CDN, YouTube watch URLs, Twitter media, and most "public-but-signed" media endpoints across the modern social web.

Fix shape: any ASR / vision / download pipeline that consumes a media URL from a third-party library MUST re-probe the LIVE listing or detail page to obtain a FRESH signed URL immediately before download. The probe-and-download workflow lives in the SAME process invocation; never split across two scheduled jobs. The kobiispy transcriber pattern: `probe_fresh_video_src(context, ad_id)` → `download_video(src, dest)` → cleanup. Both calls happen inside one Playwright session.

Detect this generally: any code that takes a media URL as an argument from an upstream cache / DB / message queue is suspicious. Audit the chain: how old can the URL be at the point it's fetched? If > 1 hour, the design is broken even when it appears to work.

### UT-14 — Sitemap `lastModified: new Date()` claims every page changed today; downranks freshness signal
Sprint 16 T1 (TUA-X) — `frontend/app/sitemap.ts` + `frontend/app/es/sitemap.ts` (created in Sprint 15 T1) emitted `lastModified: new Date()` for every URL. Effect: every Googlebot crawl saw "every marketing page changed today" → false freshness signal → wasted crawl budget → freshness/quality signal degraded over time. Worse for the per-section stories sitemaps where the fallback `a.published_at ? new Date(a.published_at) : new Date()` masked missing publish dates as "today".

Root cause: convenience default. `new Date()` is the most ergonomic choice in TypeScript and the Next 16 docs use it as the placeholder example. The fix requires explicit thought about content cadence per route.

Fix shape: keep a static `ROUTE_LAST_UPDATED: Record<string, string>` map of ISO dates, derived from `git log -1 --format=%aI -- <page.tsx>` at audit time. Update the map by hand when a route's content meaningfully changes. For dynamic listings (blog, stories), read the underlying content date (MDX frontmatter `last_updated` field, archetype `published_at`) and fall back to a CONSERVATIVE historical date — never `new Date()`. The per-blog sitemap pattern (`posts[0]?.frontmatter.last_updated ?? new Date()`) is fine ONLY because the fallback is unreachable when posts exist; if posts are empty, the sitemap is still wrong, but at least it's empty too.

Detect at smoke harness: scan `/sitemap.xml` body for `<lastmod>` entries NOT matching today's ISO date. If 100% of entries are today's date → regression (the smoke check fails). Per-section sitemaps need their own date-diversity check OR a content-driven check (e.g. fitness intelligence post must have last_updated matching its MDX `last_updated:` field). TUA-X smoke check #46 covers `/sitemap.xml`; per-section diversity is a Sprint 17 hardening candidate.

Apex 36 codifies the discipline at sprint level: any route-touching commit also bumps `ROUTE_LAST_UPDATED` for that route, same commit.

### UT-12 — Next 16 root `/sitemap.xml` 404 when `app/sitemap.ts` does not exist
Sprint 15 T1 (TUA-X) — `/sitemap.xml` and `/es/sitemap.xml` returned 404 for 4 months even though 4 per-section sitemaps (`/blog/sitemap.xml`, `/es/blog/sitemap.xml`, `/stories/sitemap.xml`, `/es/historias/sitemap.xml`) were 200. Google's first crawl by convention hits `/sitemap.xml`; the silent 404 was a coverage error that never surfaced in the smoke harness because no check covered the canonical path.

Root cause: Next 16's App Router emits `/sitemap.xml` ONLY when `frontend/app/sitemap.ts` exists. The per-section `app/<section>/sitemap.ts` files do NOT backfill the root path. The framework treats each `sitemap.ts` as a separate metadata route at the location of the file.

Fix shape: create `frontend/app/sitemap.ts` returning a `MetadataRoute.Sitemap` over the public routes, with `alternates.languages` populated for each hreflang pair. For bilingual sites, mirror at `frontend/app/<locale>/sitemap.ts` with the locale-native slugs and same alternates map.

Detect at smoke harness: probe `/sitemap.xml` + every locale-root sitemap path for status 200 AND `<loc>` substring present. The TUA-X smoke harness adds these as checks #43/#44 (root EN/ES sitemap) and #45 (robots.txt references both root sitemaps). Without the smoke check, the 404 is invisible because the per-section sitemaps mask it.

Also update `app/robots.ts`'s `sitemap` array to list the new root entries at the TOP of the list so crawlers find the bilingual canonical surface before the per-section deep dives.

### UT-13 — Public ad-library detail pages may be metadata-only; probe before designing an adapter rewrite
Sprint 15 T2 (TUA-X) — investigating "can per-ad-detail click-through capture hook_text for TikTok Ad Library?" three Playwright probes revealed: (a) the listing DOM DOES expose real `/ads/detail/?ad_id=<numeric>` URLs (contrary to a 5-day-old adapter docstring claim about "synthesised ad_id"), BUT (b) the detail page itself is metadata-only (Ad ID + dates + targeting summary + geo breakdown + advertiser info). The creative text lives in the VIDEO content, which headless Chromium renders as "Audio/video is not supported / Please TryRefresh" with NO transcript or alt text.

The Sprint 14 narrative ("DOM doesn't expose creative text") was technically right but described the wrong wall. The actual wall is the MEDIA boundary: video creative is uncapturable from the public surface without (a) video transcription, (b) frame OCR, or (c) authenticated advertiser-account Ads Manager access. None of those are cheap.

Rule: BEFORE designing or scheduling an adapter rewrite that promises to populate a NULL field via deeper navigation, run a one-shot Playwright probe (read-only, ≤90s) that navigates ONE real detail page and dumps `inner_text("body")`. If the field is not in the body — even after the deeper navigation — the rewrite cannot fulfil its premise. Lesson is structural: a one-day probe saves a one-week wasted rewrite. The TUA-X probe at `tuax_core/tools/probes/tiktok_detail_probe.py` is the reference pattern.

Detect generally: any UKDL entry that promises "this Sprint will populate X via Y rewrite" should have a probe artefact already in `vault/knowledge_base/` confirming Y produces X for at least one real input. If it doesn't, demote the work item to "research" until the probe lands.

### UT-11 — Ranker that proxies one signal scores every NULL-bearing row at zero (platform bias)
Sprint 14 T3 (TUA-X) — the `ad_signal_ingester` used `trending_score = float(len(hook_text)) if hook_text else 0.0` as a content-richness proxy. Meta Ad Library returns hook_text on every row; TikTok Ad Library's search-results DOM does not expose creative text at all (Session 28 adapter docstring confirms — only advertiser + dates + view-range bucket). Every TikTok row → score 0 → never surfaced in top-N → structural Meta bias.

Root cause: a single-proxy ranker silently encodes whichever platform happens to populate that proxy. The bias is not at the data layer (the rows are honest), nor at the ranking SQL (it's a clean ORDER BY DESC). It's at the ingester's proxy choice.

Fix shape: `compute_trending_score(hook_text, estimated_views) = max(len_score, log10(views+1)*50)`. Hybrid of content-richness (hook length) and reach lower-bound (view-range bucket). Take the max, not the sum — no double-counting. Both proxies are documented in the post itself as "content-richness" and "reach lower-bound" so they don't masquerade as CTR/ROAS (Apex 9).

Test pattern: every new adapter that can NULL the primary content proxy must accompany a reach-bucket fallback in the ingester PLUS a unit test of the shape `transform({platform: X, hook_text: None, estimated_views: K}) → score > 0`. See `tuax_core/tests/engines/test_ad_signal_ingester.py::test_transform_tiktok_bucket_lifts_score_above_zero`.

Detect this generally: if a ranker has any clause of the shape `proxy_value if X else 0`, ask "what other proxy is available when X is falsey?" — if there is one, the formula is biased.

### UT-4 — Next.js 16 + bun: `package-lock.json` NOT regenerated on `bun install`
The VPS docker build uses npm and needs `package-lock.json` present. If
`bun install` was run locally and the lock wasn't regenerated, the build fails
with "package-lock.json out of sync". Fix: `npm install --package-lock-only`

Stop-event hook fires while the agent is still mid-Edit/Write. Shell `>>` and

Visiting a second `?ref=XYZ12345` URL with an existing cookie must NOT update

### UT-7 — UnitOfWork has no `commit()` method
`async with UnitOfWork() as uow: …; await uow.commit()` raises AttributeError.
The UoW auto-commits on clean `__aexit__`. Explicit commit calls are silent
runtime bombs that only fire when an auth-gated endpoint is actually hit. Always
grep `await uow\.commit\(\)` after new router work — should be zero matches.
Sealed by SL-14 (Sprint 7) + Sprint 9 #59 (13 occurrences defused).

### UT-8 — `inserted_at` vs `created_at` schema drift
Raw-SQL migrations sometimes use `inserted_at`; the ORM models often inherit
`TimestampMixin` which provides `created_at` + `updated_at`. Constructors that
pass `inserted_at=...` will TypeError. Always check whether the model inherits
`TimestampMixin` (use `created_at`) or owns its own `inserted_at` column
(e.g. `ReferralPayout` does). Audit pattern:
`grep -rn 'inserted_at\s*=' tuax_core/{api,workers,engines}/`.

### UT-16 — Public ad-library listing DOM may expose real numeric ad_ids via anchor hrefs even when the adapter docstring claims it doesn't
Sprint 18 T1 (TUA-X / kobiispy) — the TikTok Ad Library listing DOM was claimed by the kobiispy adapter docstring (Session 28, 2026-05-25) to NOT expose the underlying ad_id, justifying a synthesised `tt-ads-dom-<hash>` ID. Sprint 16 v2 probe (read-only, 90s) inspected `page.content()` and found 12 `/ads/detail/?ad_id=<numeric>` anchor hrefs per scrape — one per card. The probe disproved the docstring claim but didn't update the code; Sprint 17's Whisper transcriber landed against synthesised IDs and silently degraded to 0 transcriptions on the existing 28 NULL TikTok rows because synth IDs don't resolve to live detail pages.

Root cause: a probe that disproves an architectural claim updates the MENTAL MODEL, not the CODE. Until someone edits the adapter, the claim still ships. The structural rule: any probe that falsifies an in-source claim MUST schedule a code-fix task in the same sprint OR explicitly mark the discovery as "verified-but-not-yet-applied" with an owner. UT-13 codified the probe-first discipline at planning time; UT-16 closes the loop at action time.

Fix shape: extract real ad_ids from listing HTML via regex (`/ads/detail/\?ad_id=(\d+)`), deduplicate preserving document order, pair with text-parsed cards positionally. Fallback to synth IDs ONLY when anchor count is short relative to card count — and surface the fallback in stats so DOM drift is visible (Apex 38 floor at >50% synth).

Detect this generally: any DOM-scraping adapter whose docstring or comment claims "the DOM does NOT expose X" should be re-probed every quarter at minimum. Public surfaces (ad libraries, marketplace listings, search results) evolve, and the prior claim's expiration is invisible until the downstream consumer breaks.

### UT-17 — Whisper transcribes platform UI voice as if it were creative; honest markers + ingester demotion prevent rank inflation
Sprint 18 T2 (TUA-X / kobiispy) — Whisper applied to TikTok ad videos cleanly transcribes the platform's own voice-over ("You've reached the cap of a thousand videos. Click to see more." in EN/FR/ES/PT). The transcripts pass the quality gate (>10 chars, alpha ratio >50%) and land in `scraped_ads.hook_text` as if they were advertiser creative. The TUA-X ingester's `compute_trending_score` then rewards them via the content-richness proxy `len(hook_text)`, inflating the rank.

Empirically: Sprint 17 batch yielded 5 system-voice transcripts out of 15 audio captures (33% contamination rate). That's a load pattern, not an edge.

Root cause: Whisper is content-agnostic. The audio source contains BOTH advertiser creative AND platform UI prompts; the transcriber emits whatever's loudest. Without language-specific pattern detection, every transcript is treated as creative.

Fix shape (two-sided): (1) transcriber emits `SYSTEM_VOICE_MARKER = '[system_voice]'` instead of the contaminated text when `is_system_voice(text)` matches any of EN/FR/ES/PT patterns. (2) ingester's `_CONTENT_EMPTY_MARKERS = frozenset({'[audio_unclear]', '[system_voice]'})` zeroes the content-richness contribution; only the reach proxy (`log10(views+1)*50`) applies. Marker literal is pinned by unit test at both sides — a rename surfaces in pytest, not at production trending_score time.

Detect this generally: any ASR / OCR / TTS pipeline applied to a platform-mediated media surface (TikTok, YouTube, Instagram Reels, Snap, X, Twitch) MUST account for the platform's voice contaminations. The patterns are platform-specific but the rule is universal: an in-process detector with explicit known-bad fixture coverage, fed by empirical observation of the first N runs. The cost of NOT having the gate is small per row but cumulative across the ranking — bias toward the platform's voice creates a tighter feedback loop than expected.

Sealed 2026-06-04 (TUA-X/KobiiSpy Sprint 40). The CW Ops intelligence pipeline

### UT-WIN-BUILD -- Windows local build/typecheck tooling is unreliable; never gate a deploy on it, and never close a turn empty after it fails
Sealed 2026-06-04 (TUA-X, Sprint 39 anti-hallucination PII closure). Three
consecutive Windows-only tooling failures burned turns and produced a
dead-screen yield. All three were ENVIRONMENT, not code:
1. `next build` (Next 16 Turbopack) COMPILES fine ("✓ Compiled successfully in
   25.4s") then the separate "Running TypeScript" worker dies natively with
   exit 3221226505 = 0xC0000409 (STATUS_STACK_BUFFER_OVERRUN). A successful
   COMPILE proves the JSX/modules are valid; the crash is the Windows TS
   worker, and the Linux build (VPS post-receive / CI) does NOT hit it.
2. `npx tsc` (and `npx <anything>`) fails with `npm error Class extends value
   undefined is not a constructor or null` -- the corepack/npx shim is broken
   on this host (already documented for pnpm; it covers tsc/eslint too).
3. `node node_modules/typescript/bin/tsc` loaded a PE binary ("This program
   cannot be run in DOS mode") -- `bin/tsc` is a shim, NOT the JS entry.

RULES (every repo, Windows host `$env:OS == "Windows_NT"`):
- NEVER gate a deploy on a local Windows `next build`. The authoritative build
  is the Linux deploy (post-receive / CI). The gate = green Turbopack COMPILE
  locally (if it even gets that far) + the Linux build + the LIVE smoke. Do not
  block the push on the Windows TS-worker crash.
- NEVER invoke `npx`/corepack on this host. Run the real JS entry directly:
  `node node_modules/typescript/lib/tsc.js --noEmit -p tsconfig.json`
  (lib/tsc.js is the entry; bin/tsc is a shim that misbehaves here).
- 2-failures law: on the SECOND env-failure of a build/typecheck tool, STOP and
  pivot to the Linux/deploy gate. Never a third shape of the same intent.

EMPTY-CLOSER BAN (the actual dead-screen this session): after the tool errors,
the turn yielded "No response requested." / a bare continue -- a ZERO-CONTENT
passive close. That IS the frozen-screen the Owner reports cross-repo. HARD: a
turn NEVER ends empty or passive after a tool failure. It ends with (1) the
failure named + its class, (2) the pivot taken, (3) the next concrete action.
This reinforces global CLAUDE.md Anti-Waiting rule (G)/(H): a sentinel, a tool
error, or a `<task-notification>` must be followed by assistant text + a
concrete next step in the SAME turn -- never a silent or content-free yield.
Reference: project `vault/governance/personal-data-audit.md` (Sprint 39).

### UT-20 — A migration that ALTERs a table holding real data follows a fixed order: backup -> migrate -> backfill -> quality-gate -> merge
Sealed 2026-06-04 (Sprint 39 niche-classifier deploy verification, TUA-X). Any
migration that ALTERs a table containing real production data follows the
mandatory order: (1) pg_dump backup verified (PR-1); (2) apply the migration
(``ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS`` is forward-compatible -- safe to apply BEFORE the
code that reads the column deploys, because the running old image ignores the new
column); (3) backfill the new column (re-run the ingester / data job); (4) run the
data-quality gate; (5) ONLY THEN merge the code that reads the column. Skipping the
quality gate before the merge ships code that reads a column whose data was never
validated -- a contaminated column looks "populated" but publishes garbage. The
gate is NOT optional. Empirical: the W23 fitness ``data_quality_gate`` correctly
EXIT 1 at purity 0.42 (<0.90) because the scraped "fitness" niche was ~58%
off-niche; the gate BLOCKING generation IS the system working, not a failure. A
re-run of the deterministic classifier cannot raise purity -- only a cleaner scrape
can. ORIGEN: HR-NICHE-01 deploy (migration 057 ``057_niche_verification.sql`` +
``niche_verified`` backfill on ``cw-postgres`` / ``tuax_mie``); identity_guard 17->0
HR-PII closure shipped same day (cfb2c90).

### UT-SHOPIFY-PUBLISH -- a real Shopify create is an external side effect; persist the store_url in the SAME transaction and reconcile via idempotency, never log the token
Sealed 2026-06-05 (Sprint 42, TUA-X). `generate_store_real` creates a REAL
product on the operator's Shopify store with real money implications. Two
structural rules the publish flow must honour:

(1) HR-6 token discipline: the decrypted access token is used only to build the
    adapter and is dropped immediately (`token = "REDACTED"`); it never enters a

(2) Create/persist atomicity + reconciliation: the Shopify create is an

    the operator has a live product with no `store_live` record. The flow runs

    SAME product (no duplicate), and the persist then lands. Idempotency is the
    reconciliation mechanism: never assume the DB write and the external create
    are atomic; make the external call replay-safe so a retry heals the gap.
