## §PROCESS-RULES (Recoverable but costly — mandatory protocol)

### PR-1 — Before any DB migration: pg_dump backup verified before applying
The dump file is the rollback substrate. Verified = restored against a scratch
DB in the same session before declaring the backup good. Per-environment:
prod gets a real verified dump; staging gets best-effort.

### PR-2 — Before updating any framework dependency (FastAPI / Next.js / SQLAlchemy / Pydantic): full test suite green on the new version
Pin-bumps that pass `pip install` but break runtime behavior (starlette
TestClient cookie semantics, Pydantic v1→v2 field defaults, Next.js 15→16
proxy.ts rename) are common. The test suite is the canary; running it BEFORE
landing the commit is the contract.

### PR-3 — Before merging to `main` from any working branch: smoke + pytest + ruff + (mypy where applicable) all green
The order matters: smoke first (cheapest, catches deploy regressions),
pytest (catches behavioral regressions), ruff/mypy (catches style/type
drift). Skipping any one of the three is a PR-3 violation.

### PR-4 — Stripe / Anthropic / Resend env vars to VPS: via SSH stdin Python only, NEVER via CLI args
The `ps aux` on a multi-user VPS reveals process args to any user. Even a
"momentary" CLI-arg containing a secret is grounds for key rotation. The
canonical pattern: base64-encode a Python script + pipe values via stdin,
NEVER `ssh user@host 'export VAR=sk_live_… && do_thing'`.
Sealed by SL-16 — Sprint 7 M9a.

### PR-5 — Local temp files containing secrets: `Path.unlink()` immediately post-use, verified via `exists()` check
Pattern: `tmp = Path(...); tmp.write_text(json.dumps({...secrets...}))` then
`tmp.unlink(); assert not tmp.exists(), "PR-5 violation: temp file still present"`.
The verification is mandatory — `unlink()` can silently no-op if the file
descriptor is open elsewhere.

### PR-7 — Before any DELETE in production of > 10 rows: pg_dump verified backup + Owner sample preview + explicit consent
Sealed 2026-05-31 (Sprint 19, TUA-X). HR-1 is the kill-switch covering ANY destructive production DB change; PR-7 codifies the procedural recipe for the common DELETE-many-rows case so the gate is mechanical, not improvised.

**The three-step contract:**
1. **Backup the affected table to disk** with a timestamped filename, captured BEFORE the DELETE statement is composed. `docker exec <pg-container> pg_dump -U <role> -d <db> -t <table> --data-only --inserts > /tmp/pre-<sprint>-<table>-<ISO8601>.sql`. Verify the dump is non-empty and parses cleanly: `wc -l <file>` returns > the table's row count (each INSERT is one line) AND `sha256sum <file>` is recorded in the sprint trace.
2. **Present the Owner with three artifacts** before requesting consent:
   - The exact SQL `WHERE` clause for the DELETE.
   - The COUNT(*) of rows that match (proves blast radius).
   - A `LIMIT 10` sample (proves the WHERE clause selects the right rows).
3. **Wait for explicit unambiguous consent** in the same session — "yes delete <name>" or equivalent. Per HR-1: implicit consent from "autonomous mode" does NOT cover this; "approved last week" does NOT cover this; the consent string must be in the current session log.

**Multi-table coupling rule:** if the DELETE target is reachable from another table through an ETL / ingester chain (e.g., kobiispy `scraped_ads` → TUA-X `ad_performance_signals`), the backup + DELETE must cover BOTH or the next ETL run re-inserts what was just deleted. Document the chain in the consent prompt so the Owner sees what's actually being touched.

**Honest-skip path:** if the Owner declines or doesn't respond inside the session, the DELETE is deferred. The agent proceeds with the rest of the sprint UNCHANGED — never partial. The sprint trace records "Owner declined, deferred" so future Sprints can pick up the cleanup with fresh consent.

**Failure mode this rule prevents:** silent over-DELETE (the WHERE clause matched more than expected), under-DELETE (orphan rows in coupled tables), unrecoverable DELETE (no backup to restore from), or pseudo-consent (agent assumed consent from generic "autonomous mode" language). Sprint 19 cleanup of 28 `tt-ads-dom-%` synth-ad_id rows from both `tuax_mie.ad_performance_signals` and `kobiispy.scraped_ads` is the reference implementation.

### PR-8 -- Before any pattern refactor (rename / replace / re-plumb): exhaustive grep, real count defines blast radius
Sealed 2026-06-03 (Sprint 37 B8, TUA-X). The brief DEFINES the scope ("change X
in 2 files"); the agent must MEASURE it. Run an exhaustive grep with NO
`--include` narrowing, excluding only build dirs (`node_modules`, `__pycache__`,
`.next`). The real match count is the blast radius. If real count > 3x the
brief's estimate -> STOP and report to the Owner before editing. Confirm every
"0 results" by re-grepping a token KNOWN to be present (a false-zero search is
worse than a positive hit). Sealed after the Sprint 37 pricing change: the brief
said "2 pages", reality was ~56 files. EP-1. See also UT-19.

### PR-9 -- Session anchor before the first tool call in any project past 10 sprints
Sealed 2026-06-03 (Sprint 37 B8, TUA-X). Long-lived projects accumulate stale
hashes / IDs / rule-numbers across sessions (EP-4 / EP-5). Before the first
substantive tool call: `git log -1` (real HEAD), newest `vault/knowledge_base/SL-*`
(last sealed state), last `Apex ` line in MEMORY.md, and the live HARD-RULES tail
(real max HR/PR/UT). If the anchor's HEAD != the HEAD assumed from the prior
session summary -> surface the discrepancy BEFORE writing code. The prior summary
is a hint; the repo is the truth. Full ritual: project `vault/governance/SESSION_PREFLIGHT.md`.
Empirical: the Sprint 37 brief quoted "UT-62..65 / PR-14/15" while the live file
was at UT-17 / PR-7 -- the new entries appended at the real next slots instead.

### PR-10 -- Every ORM table the cw surface uses at runtime MUST be registered in `scripts/cw_init_db.py` `_CW_TABLES`
Sealed 2026-06-03 (Sprint 35 Bloque 0, TUA-X). The cw_postgres bootstrap creates
ONLY the tables listed in `_CW_TABLES` -- the alembic chain assumes legacy raw-SQL
tables that don't exist on a fresh cw_postgres, so it is bypassed for the cw
surface. The local SQLite test tier runs `create_all` over the FULL ORM metadata,
so a missing `_CW_TABLES` entry is INVISIBLE locally and 500s every endpoint that
touches the table on prod. Before shipping any router that reads/writes a NEW
model: add `("<table>", Model.__table__)` to `_CW_TABLES` in CREATE-dependency
order (FK parents first; order-free if no FK). Verify in the cw_migrate log:
`[cw_init_db] <table>: ok`. This is the SAME class as the "PR-14 lesson"
referenced verbatim in cw_init_db.py comments -- but PR-14 never existed in the
live registry (EP-4 phantom ID); PR-10 is the real slot. Sealed after
`operator_tenant` (+ same sprint `operator_profile`, `shopify_credential`) were
absent from `_CW_TABLES` and would have 500'd every re-plumbed operator endpoint
on cw_postgres (the re-plumb made previously DB-free endpoints open a UoW). Pairs
with PR-11.

### PR-11 -- The `deploy/post-receive` hook MUST name `cw_migrate` in the `docker compose up --build` set so the bootstrap rebuilds with the pushed code before cw_api boots
Sealed 2026-06-03 (Sprint 35 Bloque 0, TUA-X). `cw_api depends_on cw_migrate`
with `condition: service_completed_successfully` orders the START, but
`depends_on` does NOT force a rebuild. If the hook runs
`up -d --build cw_api cw_frontend` (omitting cw_migrate), compose reuses
cw_migrate's STALE image -> the one-shot runs OLD `cw_init_db.py` that doesn't
know the new table -> the table is never created -> cw_api 500s even though the
new code is on disk. Fix: `up -d --build cw_migrate cw_api cw_frontend` (migrate
named FIRST). Window-free + idempotent (`checkfirst=True`). PR-10 puts the table
in the bootstrap; PR-11 guarantees the bootstrap that runs is built from the
pushed code. Same dormant-deploy class as the X.156 (docker), X.157 (landing),
and Sprint 6.1 (nginx) hook fixes -- code on disk that never reaches the runtime.

### PR-12 -- DATASET-FIRST: read recent vault extraction / audit docs BEFORE declaring any knowledge or functionality "absent" or "to build from scratch"
Sealed 2026-06-07 (Sprint Knowledge-Integration, TUA-X). Before diagnosing that a
capability, framework, or piece of knowledge does NOT exist in the system, the
agent MUST read the recent project knowledge artifacts:
`vault/knowledge_base/cw-ops-knowledge-extraction-*.md` and `vault/audits/*.md`
dated within the last ~14 days. These documents already enumerate documented gaps
AND (often) the exact code that implements them.

rules.title: an operator credential is honest only when a real consumer reads it; "configured" != "verified"
rules.trigger:
building or extending any operator-facing "connect / paste your key"
surface.

any operator integration (Shopify, Meta, TikTok, UGC, ...) or any
"is this connected" surface.

any "not configured" / 503 / ``is_configured() == False`` where the
credential is confirmed PRESENT in the host ``.env``.

adding or changing any pricing tier, checkout plan, or the
checkout->webhook->provision chain.

any code that folds a test outcome into ``niche_intelligence_moat``
(``record_outcome``), or any "auto-capture from connected APIs" path.

any code that emits a batch of ad creatives (the awareness creative
suggester, any future per-operator creative generator).

adding or tuning any LLM cache key.

any creative-generation path for a niche that exists in the vault.

any auto-kill / circuit-breaker that is supposed to stop real spend.

any kill decision that consumes a conversion-derived metric.
rules.stop:
(1) store encrypted via vault_cipher, never plaintext; (2) the plaintext
is decrypted ONLY transiently inside an authenticated operator request and is
NEVER logged/returned (HR-6) -- prefer header auth (``Authorization: Bearer``)
over a URL query param so the token cannot leak into httpx request logs; (3) a
real consumer of the stored value MUST exist before the surface ships (a live
verify call, an engine, a worker). If the only consumer is future, say so in the
copy and do NOT imply the capability is active.

expose a status endpoint returning ``configured`` + ``verified``
(operator-scoped, 401 unauth). ``verified`` must reflect a real API call -- the
migration-free signal is ``status=="active"`` AND ``last_used_at`` stamped, set by
the verify endpoint, NOT a manual toggle and NOT a decrypt check. Surface the
distinction on the dashboard (green=verified / amber=configured-unverified /
grey=missing) with a CTA when missing.

verify the RUNNING container's env, not just the file:
``docker exec <c> printenv VAR`` (HR-6: for a secret print only ``| wc -c`` length
or the public first-8, never the value). If the var is absent from the process,
add ``VAR: ${VAR:-}`` to that service's ``environment:`` block (repo-tracked
compose -> commit -> deploy so it is durable, not a VPS-only edit the next
``git checkout -f`` survives but a future compose change reverts) and recreate the
container. Do NOT assume a missing secret and do NOT reach for a vendor CLI to
"re-provision" a credential that already exists -- the gap is local config.

ship all three legs in one commit/deploy. Verify each leg with its own
gate -- a SMOKE that asserts the wired CTA renders (leg 2, e.g. the per-plan
``data-testid`` is in the rendered HTML, never a hollow card) AND a UNIT TEST
that the webhook records the right plan + provisions the right profile for that
plan (leg 3). Never deploy the card before the webhook consumes the plan. Do NOT
create live Stripe Checkout sessions on every CI smoke run (one-time manual probe
for "no 503"); do NOT fake a signed-webhook smoke (unit-test the handler instead).

the moat is fed ONLY from (a) a verified-API read (Shopify revenue +
Meta/TikTok spend) OR (b) an explicit operator self-report (``source=
manual_self_reported``). The automatic path is GATED twice -- verified ad APIs
connected AND a real test->ad->spend linkage that yields verified numbers -- and
when either gate is unmet it returns a dormant reason and writes NOTHING (never
synthesises a number to "fill in"). The simulated / PAL / dry-run path NEVER
reaches the moat. UT-MOAT-ANONYMITY companion: only an anonymized ``TestOutcome``
(niche + economics, NO tenant) crosses; the ``test_results`` tenant id physically
cannot reach the moat (the moat table has no operator column).

derive each card's hook_type from its SOURCE archetype (vault hooks
library category via ``loader.get_hooks_tagged``), then select round-robin across
archetypes so a batch of >=2 cards spans >=2 distinct hook_types. Never assign one
awareness-level hook_type to every card in a batch.

every cache row carries ``task_type`` + ``model_used`` + ``hit_count``;
the admin stats break hit_rate down so drift is visible. If hit_rate < 20% after
30 days the keys are too specific (e.g. a volatile field leaked into the key) ->
widen them. Never ship a cache whose effectiveness cannot be read back.

read ``knowledge/vault/hooks_library.yaml`` (via ``loader.get_hooks_tagged``)
first; rank by ``template_performance`` win_rate (``engines/first_win/template_ranking``);
only fall through to an LLM when the vault has no template for the niche -- and when
it does, the fix is to ADD the template to the YAML, not to silently spend on the
LLM. The performance ledger learns from REAL closed tests only (dormant feed,
UT-NO-FABRICATED-FEED) -- never fabricated win data.

the kill executor (``kill_executor.evaluate_and_kill``) MUST pause the
campaign on the operator's OWN account (HR-6 header auth) and flip the row to
``killed`` with the reason. The done-gate is a test that simulates CPA > 2x target
and asserts BOTH the campaign reads PAUSED in the ``httpx.MockTransport`` response
AND the ``ad_executions`` row reads ``killed`` -- never just that the decision
function returns ``should_kill``. The row is marked killed even if the pause API
call fails (``paused=False`` surfaces the retry), so it never reads falsely
"running".

the conversion refresh records ``conversions=None`` (not 0) on an
unverifiable tick; ``fwe_kill_check`` carries a ``conversions_observed`` flag so
the NO_CONVERSION rule fires ONLY on a verified real zero, and the CPA/ROAS rules
are skipped when their metric is None. Spend-cap + daily-cap kills (which need no
conversion data) always remain available. A simulated/None-derived CPA kill is
worse than no CPA kill.
rules.evidence:
Sprint 45 -- the Meta/TikTok ad-launch + spend-pull engines read ONE
SYSTEM credential set (``Settings().meta_ads_*`` / ``tiktok_ads_*``) over a single
``brand_id="default"``; no path fed a per-operator vault token into the launcher.
Storing an operator's ad token would have been a scaffold EXCEPT the live verify
endpoint (Meta ``/me/adaccounts``, TikTok ``/advertiser/info/``) is a genuine
consumer. Per-operator autonomous execution was deferred to Sprint 46 with honest
"stored and verified -- ready for activation" copy, never "we're running your ads".

### UT-INTEGRATION-STATUS -- every external operator integration exposes a status that distinguishes "configured" from "verified"
Sealed 2026-06-05 (Sprint 45, TUA-X). ``configured`` = the credential exists in
the vault. ``verified`` = the credential was tested against the REAL external API
and worked. A system that assumes a stored key is valid without verifying it fails
silently when the operator tries to execute. The decrypt-only "test" (does the
ciphertext decrypt) is NOT verification.

Sprint 45 -- the ``api_credentials`` vault ``/test`` only proved
decryptability; nothing called the real API. The verify endpoints close that gap.
Smoke [105]-[108] guard that the status + verify endpoints are mounted +
operator-gated (401, never 404).


### UT-ENV-ALLOWLIST-DRIFT -- a secret present in .env but absent from a container's explicit `environment:` allowlist reads as EMPTY in-process; "not configured"/503 with the secret present is a passthrough gap, not a missing secret
Sealed 2026-06-05 (Sprint 46 ops, TUA-X). When a docker-compose service declares
an explicit ``environment:`` block and NO ``env_file:``, ONLY the vars listed in
that block reach the process. Adding a var to ``.env`` is INVISIBLE to the
container until it is ALSO added to the ``environment:`` allowlist. The symptom is
an endpoint reporting "not configured" / returning 503 even though
``grep VAR .env`` shows the value is present -- the file has it, the process does
not.

2026-06-05 -- Shopify ``/connect`` 503'd in prod and ``/shopify/status``
was ``{configured:false}``. ``SHOPIFY_API_KEY`` + ``SHOPIFY_API_SECRET`` were both
already in ``working/.env`` but ABSENT from cw_api's ``environment:`` allowlist
(no ``env_file:``), so ``shopify_oauth.is_configured()`` read empty. The brief
assumed "the secret is not in .env, re-provision via the shopify CLI" -- BOTH
premises false. Fix: wire the two vars (plus ``STRIPE_PRICE_ID_FWE``) into the
cw_api ``environment:`` block + redeploy; zero CLI, zero browser auth. Detect at
the smoke harness: ``/api/v1/shopify/status`` -> ``{"configured":true}`` (smoke
[109], asserts configured:true specifically, not just the substring). Project:
``vault/knowledge_base/SL-66-sprint46-ops-stripe-shopify-config.md``.


### UT-PRICING-PARITY -- a new pricing tier needs THREE legs deployed together; (1)+(2) without (3) charges money without granting the right access
Sealed 2026-06-05 (Sprint 46, TUA-X). Every new pricing tier (or checkout plan)
is incomplete by definition until all three legs ship in ONE cutover:
  (1) BACKEND: the Stripe ``price_id`` exists in prod ``.env`` AND the checkout
      endpoint selects it from the plan the visitor chose;
  (2) FRONTEND: a plan card on ``/pricing`` (and every locale mirror) with a
      REAL wired CTA that POSTs the plan -- not copy, not a dead button;
  (3) WEBHOOK: ``checkout.session.completed`` reads ``metadata.plan`` and
      provisions the CORRECT tier/profile + records the correct subscription
      (plan_name / amount / price_id).
A tier with only (1) is an orphan backend. A tier with (1)+(2) but NOT (3) is the
worst failure: it CHARGES MONEY and then provisions the wrong access level (the
cheaper buyer recorded as the dearer plan, or vice-versa) -- silent, and only
discovered when a real customer is mis-served.

2026-06-05 -- the €12 First Win Engine price (leg 1) had existed since
Sprint 44, but the deployed ``/pricing`` had NO FWE card (leg 2) and the webhook
IGNORED ``metadata.plan`` -- the checkout wrote it, nothing read it -- so every
buyer was recorded as the €49 Operator with no ``first_win_profile`` (leg 3).
A €12 buyer would have paid €12 and been provisioned as a €49 operator. All three
legs shipped together in Sprint 46. Detect at the smoke harness: ``/pricing`` +
``/es/precios`` carry both ``data-testid=checkout-submit-{fwe,operator}`` CTAs
(smoke [110]/[111]); the webhook plan-branch is unit-tested
(``test_webhook_persistence.py`` Sprint 46 block). Project:
``vault/knowledge_base/SL-67-sprint46-fwe-frontend-tier.md``.


### UT-NO-FABRICATED-FEED -- the collective moat is fed ONLY from real verified or operator-declared outcomes; a dormant auto-feed must be structurally unable to write fabricated data
Sealed 2026-06-05 (Sprint 46b, TUA-X). The First Win moat compounds across every
operator -- so a single fabricated/simulated row poisons the intelligence for
ALL future operators, permanently. The feed must therefore be incapable of
writing anything but real data, not merely "careful not to".

2026-06-05 -- Sprint 46b wired the test-capture feed
(``test_results_repo``). Per-operator ad execution does not exist yet, so the
auto-feed is DORMANT: ``attempt_auto_capture`` returns ``no_verified_ad_apis`` /
``linkage_pending`` and writes nothing -- proven by
``test_v_no_fabricated_feed_without_apis`` + ``test_v_auto_feed_dormant_even_with_apis``.
The moat grows today only from an explicit operator close
(``POST /api/v1/fwe/test/{id}/close``). Project:
``vault/knowledge_base/SL-68-sprint46b-collective-intelligence.md``.


### UT-ANDROMEDA -- a creative batch of 3+ variants must carry >=2 distinct hook_types, or Meta Andromeda collapses them under one entity-ID and yields no independent learning
Sealed 2026-06-06 (Sprint 47, TUA-X). Meta's 2026 Andromeda retrieval groups
near-duplicate variants (same persona / message / hook archetype / format) under
a single entity-ID. A batch where every card shares one hook_type therefore
returns one signal, not N -- the test spends N budgets to learn once.

awareness spec's single hook_type to EVERY card (the exact Andromeda collapse).
Fixed with ``get_hooks_tagged`` + ``_diverse_select``. Detect:

batch. Project: ``vault/knowledge_base/SL-69-sprint47-validation-intelligence.md``.


### UT-CACHE-HIT -- the LLM cache must expose a traceable hit_count + measurable hit_rate; a cache without metrics cannot prove it is saving money, and over-specific keys silently never hit
Sealed 2026-06-06 (Sprint 48, TUA-X). A cache that no one can measure is faith,
not engineering. ``GET /api/v1/admin/llm-cache-stats`` (owner-gated) returns
hit_rate + total_calls + estimated_cost_saved so the saving is observed, not
asserted.

(reset every deploy, unmeasurable) with the durable ``llm_response_cache`` +

### UT-TEMPLATE-FIRST -- the creative engine consults the vault hook library (and its win_rate ranking) BEFORE any LLM call; an LLM call for a documented niche means the YAML is incomplete, not that the LLM is needed
Sealed 2026-06-06 (Sprint 48, TUA-X). The cheapest LLM call is the one not made.
The operator creative path is deterministic (vault templates, ``llm_called=False``)
and now ordered by historical win_rate -- a documented niche should never need an
LLM round-trip.

2026-06-06 -- Sprint 48 added ``template_performance`` + win_rate ranking
in the suggester (empty ledger -> vault order, graceful). Detect:
``test_v_rank_tagged_prefers_winner`` + ``test_v_feed_from_test_results_is_dormant``.
Project: SL-70.


### UT-KILL-VERIFIED -- the automatic kill must be test-verifiable; a kill that cannot be confirmed as PAUSED in a mock is not implemented
Sealed 2026-06-07 (Sprint 49, TUA-X). ``fwe_kill_check`` returns a pure DECISION;
the kill is real only when an ACTION pauses the live ad. It is tempting to ship
the decision and call the kill "done" -- but a decision that never reaches the
platform leaves money burning.

2026-06-07 -- Sprint 49 B6. Detect:
``test_v_kill_cpa_2x_pauses_and_marks_killed``. Project: SL-71.


### UT-KILL-CPA-REAL -- a CPA/ROAS kill fires ONLY on verified conversion data; an unverifiable read falls back to spend-only kills, never a fabricated zero
Sealed 2026-06-07 (Sprint 50, TUA-X). The conversion-driven kills (CPA > 2x
target, ROAS < 1.9x after a fair run) depend on real spend + real conversions.
If conversions can't be read (no connected store, Shopify outage), the engine
must NOT invent a zero -- a zero would trip the NO_CONVERSION kill on a healthy

rules.title: an OAuth-capable platform MUST connect via OAuth; a manual token-paste field is a friction + security antipattern

### UT-SELECT-NATIVE-CROSS-OS (a.k.a. TRAP-SELECT-NATIVE) -- native <select>/<option> rendering varies by OS and browser; a select legible on macOS can be invisible on Chrome Windows, so a native select is never "done" until verified on the dev's NON-dev OS

is drawn by the OS/browser, not fully by the page CSS: Chrome on macOS uses a
system-white popup background, Chrome on Windows inherits the `<select>`'s own
background, and Firefox/Safari differ again. An `<option>` that relies on an
implicit (inherited) background is therefore legible on one OS and invisible on
another -- the most dangerous kind of bug because it passes the developer's own
screen and only breaks for the user.

RECOGNISE: a native `<select>`/`<option>` whose options omit an explicit
`background-color`, especially on a dark-theme form. If the project has ANY
Windows users (every Owner project does), verifying the dropdown only on the
dev OS is insufficient.

option declare explicit `color` + `background-color`; the dropdown is opened in

Project: CW Ops /waitlist. Pairs with HR-SELECT-CONTRAST.
