HR-PROFIT-MATURITY. Project: SL-100.
rules.exception:
none. A store with no active credential, or one synced within the window, raises
no alert -- the absence of a gap is honest, never suppressed.

rules.title: the system never mixes USD and EUR without verified conversion or an explicit label; a 20% computed on an un-normalized mixed-currency base is forbidden
rules.trigger:
any settlement of the revenue share that subtracts ad spend from gross revenue (the
GET /billing/revenue-share endpoint, ``_build_period_records``), or any change to the
currency-mismatch gate.
rules.stop:
ad spend is recorded in USD
(``AdSpendRecord.spend_usd``) and Shopify gross is EUR. With no FX conversion in the system, if
there is REAL USD ad spend (> 0) to subtract, set ``currency_mismatch=True``, build NO profit
record, and bill 0 -- surfacing both labelled figures (``gross_eur`` / ``ad_spend_usd``) -- never
an approximate charge on a guessed/hardcoded FX rate. A period with zero USD spend has no mixing
and settles normally.
rules.evidence:
2026-06-11 SL-101 (shipped by the currency pane; the revenue-ledger pane's parallel
pref-based variant was abandoned in its favour) -- pricing_router subtracted ad spend (USD) from
Shopify gross (EUR) without conversion, silently mixing currencies and mis-sizing the 20%. The
``currency_mismatch`` gate (any real USD spend vs EUR gross -> €0 this period) closes it.
Affects api/pricing_router.py, db/models/user_preference.py. Detect: smoke [245] +
tests/api/test_d08_currency.py. Pairs with HR-BILLING-MATURITY + HR-BILLING-GRANULARITY.
rules.exception:
none. No invented FX rate is ever used to bridge a mismatch; the only paths are "zero
USD spend -> settle normally" or "real USD spend vs EUR gross -> flag and wait for a real
conversion".

rules.title: the 20% base must be as granular as the data allows; the per-order ledger is the source of truth and the monthly total is only a fallback, never the reverse
rules.trigger:
any code that sources the gross for a billing period (the revenue-share endpoint), or any
change to the per-order ledger sync / repo / the order-vs-monthly preference order.
rules.stop:
prefer ``revenue_order_ledger.sum_for_period`` when
it has the period's orders (order-level sum + the REAL order currency + ``order_count``); fall back
to the live trailing read ONLY when the ledger is empty (first sync / no orders). Never prefer the
monthly proxy over real per-order data. The sync upserts each paid order idempotently (unique on
``shopify_order_id``) so a re-sync never double-counts.
rules.evidence:
2026-06-11 SL-101 -- the revenue sync stored only a monthly total, so the 20% base was a
period proxy. ``revenue_order_ledger`` + the billing preference give an order-level base that
sharpens automatically as the ledger fills. Affects db/models/revenue_order_ledger.py,
db/repositories/revenue_order_ledger_repo.py, integrations/shopify_revenue.py,
workers/tasks/sync_shopify_revenue.py, api/pricing_router.py. Detect: smoke [246]-[248] +
tests/billing/test_revenue_order_ledger.py. Pairs with HR-BILLING-MATURITY + HR-CURRENCY-HONEST.
rules.exception:
the monthly/live read is used ONLY as the fallback when the ledger has no rows for the
period -- never chosen over a populated ledger.

rules.title: the FX rate that normalizes a mixed-currency billing base is an Owner-set monthly constant (ECB published), NEVER a live external API; no rate set -> billing stays halted, never a guessed value
rules.trigger:
any code that converts between currencies inside the billing / revenue-share path, or any
new source of an FX rate (a table, a config, an integration, an API client) feeding a money figure.
rules.stop:
read the rate ONLY from the Owner-set
``fx_rate`` table (``FxRateRepository.get_rate`` for the billing month) via the pure
``engines/pricing/fx.convert_usd_to_eur`` (EUR = USD ÷ EUR/USD rate, guards a non-positive rate ->
None). If ``get_rate`` returns None, DO NOT convert and DO NOT bill -- keep the period
``currency_mismatch`` exactly as before. Never call a live FX API. Never seed/default a rate into
the billing path. The Owner sets the rate at the owner-gated ``PUT /api/v1/admin/billing-settings/
fx-rate`` (require_owner; OWNER_EMAILS allowlist).
rules.evidence:
2026-06-11 SL-102 -- HR-CURRENCY-HONEST correctly halted the 20% for USD-spend/EUR-gross
operators, but that left them un-billable. The honest unlock is an Owner-controlled monthly ECB
constant, not a live rate a billing fee should never rest on. Affects db/models/fx_rate.py,
db/repositories/fx_rate_repo.py, engines/pricing/fx.py, api/admin_billing_settings_router.py,
api/pricing_router.py, scripts/cw_init_db.py. Runbook: vault/governance/ecb-fx-rate-runbook.md.
Detect: smoke [249]-[251] + tests/billing/test_fx_rate.py. Pairs with HR-CURRENCY-HONEST +
HR-BILLING-MATURITY + HR-BILLING-GRANULARITY.
rules.exception:
a live/automated FX source is permitted ONLY behind an explicit Owner confirmation step
that writes the confirmed value into ``fx_rate`` -- the billing path still reads the Owner-set row,
never the API directly.

rules.title: the ECB rate applies ONLY to the billing month it was set for; a rate from month M is never used to convert month M-1 / M+1; no rate for the billing period -> currency_mismatch
rules.trigger: any code that looks up or applies an FX rate inside the billing / revenue-share path.
rules.stop:
look the rate up by the BILLING period's month
(``FxRateRepository.get_rate(valid_month=period)``), never by "the latest" or "any available"
rate. If no rate exists for that exact month, do NOT borrow a neighbouring month's rate — keep the
period ``currency_mismatch`` (HR-CURRENCY-HONEST). This guard is already enforced by the
``valid_month=period`` keying in ``pricing_router._build_period_records``; never weaken it to a
"most recent rate" fallback.
rules.evidence:
2026-06-12 SL-103 -- audit before real operators: a wrong-month rate would silently bill
the 20% on a net normalised at the wrong exchange rate (months differ by points). The lookup keys
on ``valid_month=period`` so a month-M rate is invisible to a month-(M-1) billing. Affects
api/pricing_router.py, db/repositories/fx_rate_repo.py. Detect: smoke [260] +
tests/billing/test_fx_rate.py::test_billing_rate_from_wrong_month_never_used. Pairs with
HR-FX-RATE-OWNER-CONTROLLED + HR-CURRENCY-HONEST.
rules.exception:
none. A rate is per-month or it is not used. (A future explicit Owner-confirmed
"carry-forward last month's rate" would have to write a real row for the new month, not borrow.)

rules.title: the operator can always see the state of their OWN billing; pending / no-sales / currency-mismatch are valid states shown honestly, never a bare 0 or an error, and never another operator's data
rules.trigger:
any surface that shows (or any endpoint that returns) an operator's billing / revenue-share
state; any change to who can read the revenue-share endpoint.
rules.stop:
the read endpoint MUST be operator-accessible
(``require_subscriber_or_operator``, not owner-only) and MUST scope to the caller's OWN identity
(``_tenant(auth)`` -> the session email, never a request-body tenant, never another operator). The
UI MUST render every state honestly: CALCULADO (the fee + the verified base + order count),
PENDIENTE (the amount in the refund window + an honest availability estimate), SIN_DATOS ("sin ventas
todavía", never €0,00 as if billed), CURRENCY_MISMATCH (waiting on the ECB rate, non-alarming). A
fetch failure shows an amiable card, never a raw "Failed to fetch".
rules.evidence:
2026-06-12 SL-104 -- the Owner could see every operator's 20% state but the operator saw
NOTHING (no fee, no base, no pending). The revenue-share endpoint was already operator-accessible +
tenant-isolated; the gap was purely the operator-facing view. Affects api/pricing_router.py
(revenue_share endpoint), frontend/app/app/configuracion (RevenueShareSection), frontend/lib/
revenueShare.ts. Detect: smoke [264]-[265] + tests/billing/test_fx_rate.py::
test_revenue_share_operator_sees_only_own_data. Pairs with HR-CURRENCY-HONEST + HR-BILLING-MATURITY.
rules.exception:
aggregate/cross-operator billing views are OWNER-only (require_owner) -- an operator sees
only their own. The Owner console (HR-FX-RATE-OWNER-CONTROLLED surfaces) is the separate owner lane.

rules.title: the operator can always see the state of their OWN subscription: plan, next-payment date, and whether a payment is pending; the date is the real persisted value or honestly absent, never invented
rules.trigger:
any surface that shows (or any endpoint that returns) an operator's subscription / plan
state; any change to who can read the subscription endpoint.
rules.stop:
the read endpoint MUST be operator-accessible
(``require_subscriber_or_operator``) and scope to the caller's OWN identity (``_tenant(auth)`` -> the
session email, never a request-body tenant). It MUST be served from the local Subscription mirror
(webhook-persisted current_period_end + status) -- NO live Stripe call on a per-operator GET. The
next-payment date is the real persisted value or honestly absent ("se confirmará en breve" when the
customer.subscription.updated event has not yet landed) -- NEVER a fabricated date. No subscription ->
200 with status 'none' (an invited/pre-launch operator), NEVER a 404. The UI renders every state with
honest, specific copy + a working CTA where one is needed (past_due/canceled/none -> a REAL existing
route, never a broken billing-portal link the system does not have).
rules.evidence:
2026-06-12 SL-105 -- SL-104 gave the operator their 20% revenue-share view but left the plan
state (active/expired + next-payment) invisible because current_period_end lived in an owner-only
``SubscriptionAdminOut``. The data was already webhook-persisted on the Subscription row; the gap was
purely the operator-facing endpoint + view. Affects api/pricing_router.py (my_subscription endpoint),
engines/entitlement/resolver.py (reused), frontend/app/app/configuracion (SubscriptionSection),
frontend/lib/subscription.ts. Detect: smoke [266]-[269] + tests/billing/test_subscription_view.py +
frontend __tests__/SubscriptionSection.test.tsx. Pairs with HR-BILLING-TRANSPARENCY + the D12
entitlement drift (subscription expiry -> kill ads).
rules.exception:
a field the local mirror does not persist (e.g. cancel_at_period_end) is OMITTED, never
surfaced as a constant stand-in value -- a fabricated ``false`` would be a lie the moment a sub is
scheduled to cancel at period end.

rules.title: the shadow-run never executes a real-world action; it reads, analyses, generates drafts, and computes recommendations, but never publishes to Shopify, spends on Meta, kills live ads, or emails customers
rules.trigger: any change to loop_console_router (the 6-step shadow pipeline) or any new step added to it.
rules.stop:
each step may READ data, run a PURE / dry-run engine
(WDE evaluate, SGE generate_store(dry_run=True), CIE generate), or COMPUTE a recommendation
(decide_kill_or_scale returns a decision, it never executes it). A step that would require a
real-world action MUST return the recommendation of what the system WOULD do -- never the action.
Forbidden in any shadow step: Shopify publish, Meta ad spend, killing live ads, sending customer
emails. The campaign step is the SHADOW PAL (sim_scenario) -- it simulates platform responses, it
never calls a live ad API. Every step also declares data_source ("engine_real" | "simulation") so
the operator sees which engines are real and which are simulated -- honesty over the appearance of
full autonomy.
rules.evidence:
2026-06-12 SL-106 -- the shadow-run already composed 4 real engines (WDE / SGE-dry / CIE /
kill-scale) + 2 honest simulations (seeded scan, PAL campaign), but nothing LABELLED which was
which, so a viewer (the investor demo) could not tell real from simulated. Build B added the
data_source label and this invariant. Affects api/loop_console_router.py, components/molecules/
SovereignLoopConsole.tsx. Detect: smoke [279]-[280] + tests/api/test_loop_console_router.py::
test_run_shadow_steps_label_real_vs_simulation. The dry_run=True (SGE) + sim_scenario (PAL) are the
two load-bearing guards. Pairs with HR-PUBLISH (publish needs 3 gates) + the D12 entitlement kill.
rules.exception:
writing the run's OWN result rows (LoopRun, demo-tagged ad_spend telemetry, internal
tickets) is allowed -- those are how the shadow result is persisted and displayed, not real-world
side effects on the operator's store or ad accounts.

rules.title: no experiment in CW Ops spends capital without the ECE evaluating a Prior Knowledge Map first; the PKM is the prerequisite of any ad spend; a correct ECE BLOCK is absolute, while an internal ECE error fails open
rules.trigger:
any code path that confirms/creates a paid test plan or otherwise commits ad capital
(fwe_router.confirm_launch_test and any future per-operator launcher).
rules.stop:
build the Prior Knowledge Map (5 real memory sources:
niche_priors, causal_memory, moat, failure_patterns, loader) + run decide_compression BETWEEN the
pre-market gate verdict and plan creation. A correct BLOCK means the experiment does NOT run under any
circumstance, even if the operator insists. The ONLY relief is fail-open on an INTERNAL ECE error: if
build_prior_knowledge_map / decide_compression raises, log it and let the launch continue -- an ECE bug
must never block the capital flow. The PKM is tenant-neutral by schema (moat + CPM carry no operator
column, HR-COLLECTIVE-DATA); the decision persists with the authenticated tenant_email.
rules.evidence:
2026-06-16 SL-116 -- ECE v1 (PKM + compression_decisions + L0-L3 ladder + learning density)
wired into confirm_launch_test. Substrate: engines/ece/{prior_knowledge_map,compression_engine,
learning_density}.py + compression_decision_repo + the experiment_compressed Karma event. Verified on
prod HEAD 8ec04e1: 25/25 pytest + live smoke 305/305; STOP-1 latency 2.66ms (< 200ms). Pairs with
HR-VALIDATION-GATE (the gate runs first) + HR-ECE-BLOCK-IS-VALUE.
rules.exception:
none for skipping the PKM. "Autonomous mode" does not authorize spending without it -- the
PKM IS the precondition. Fail-open covers only an internal ECE error, never a deliberate skip.

rules.title: an ECE BLOCK with a specific reason_code is a productive output, not a failure; the capital it protected is shown to the operator in human language, always in euros; a BLOCK without a specific reason is noise disguised as protection
rules.trigger:
any ECE decision surfaced to the operator (the experiment_compressed Karma event, any future
ECE diagnostic surface).
rules.stop:
every BLOCK/COMPRESS carries a specific reason_code (FP-X,
CLAIM_RISK_HIGH, MOAT_FAILED_PRODUCT, LOW_LEARNING, MICRO_TEST...) rendered as a concrete Spanish Karma
message ending in the capital protected/saved in euros ("Bloquee el test antes de gastar. ... Capital
protegido: EUR40"). A RUN emits NO event (no noise). A message with no concrete reason is suppressed
(build_message returns None) rather than shipped generic (UT-KARMA-SPECIFIC-MESSAGES). expected_savings_eur
is derived from the real proposed budget, never invented (HR-VAULT-PRIOR).
rules.evidence:
2026-06-16 SL-116 -- the experiment_compressed event (engines/karma/messages._experiment_compressed)
+ the reason_codes on compression_decisions. Verified: test_ece_karma_and_latency (message carries the
reason + euros; skips without a reason). Pairs with HR-KARMA-IDENTITY + HR-KARMA-TONE + HR-ECE-PRIOR-MANDATORY.
rules.exception: none. A BLOCK without a specific reason equals not having blocked anything.

rules.title: the creative batch producer never delivers a combination whose hook_text still carries a literal field token; if the operator's product lacks the data a hook needs, that hook is OMITTED, never shipped with a literal token
rules.trigger:
engines/ece/creative_batch.generate_combinations (or any future caller that fills hook templates
from a product hypothesis).
rules.stop:
a hook_text is emitted ONLY when EVERY {field} of its template is fillable
from the operator's real product hypothesis (the string.Formatter field set is a subset of the fill map).
A template needing a field the product lacks is OMITTED; the batch volume shrinks honestly rather than ship
an unfilled token. A final defensive guard drops any hook_text still containing '{' or '['. HIGH claim_risk
archetypes are pre-filtered (the EGO ads gate already hard-blocks them, HR-EGO-PESOS).
rules.evidence:
2026-06-16 SL-117 -- BL-KI-08 (false prompt premise: a batch_producer already existed shipping RAW
templates; this module FILLS them). Verified: test_creative_batch (no '{' or '[' in any hook_text; a thin
product omits the templates it cannot fill). Pairs with HR-NO-FABRICATED-COPY + HR-EGO-PESOS.
rules.exception:
none. A batch with a literal field token in the delivered copy is broken; reduce the volume,
never the honesty.

rules.title: the creative batch is pre-filtered by the ECE before the operator sees it; the operator gets an intelligence-ranked menu, never the raw cross-product of every possible option
rules.trigger: engines/ece/creative_batch.filter_and_rank / produce_batch (the operator-facing creative menu).
rules.stop:
every combination is run through decide_compression; BLOCKed combinations
(a product the moat saw fail, a HIGH claim-risk hook, or a LOW_LEARNING re-test of an already-understood
niche -- Core law 13) are DROPPED, never shown. Survivors are ordered by learning density (COMPOUND ->
STRATEGIC -> USEFUL) so the operator's attention goes to the highest-learning tests first. The count
filtered is reported (transparency), never hidden.
rules.evidence:
2026-06-16 SL-117 -- BL-KI-08, built on the SL-116 ECE. Verified: test_creative_batch (a hook
archetype the moat already knows wins at HIGH confidence is removed as LOW_LEARNING before the menu, while
novel archetypes survive). Pairs with HR-ECE-PRIOR-MANDATORY + HR-ECE-BLOCK-IS-VALUE.
rules.exception: none. Presenting the raw unfiltered cross-product is presenting noise.

rules.title: the tests-sugeridos surface always shows the operator HOW MANY options the system filtered and WHY; the ECE's intelligence is a visible feature, never invisible
rules.trigger:
any operator-facing surface that renders the ECE-filtered creative batch (the /app tests-sugeridos
section; engines/ece/creative_batch via GET /api/v1/fwe/creative-batch).
rules.stop:
when the ECE filtered any combination (filtered_out > 0), the surface states
the count and the reason ("El sistema filtro N opciones que ya conoce como poco efectivas en <niche>") in
plain language. An undocumented niche shows the pkm_is_guessing honesty notice. The filter count is never
hidden to make the menu look cleaner.
rules.evidence:
2026-06-16 SL-118 -- the creative-batch endpoint (filtered_out + pkm_is_guessing in the response) +
the TestsSugeridos surface. Pairs with HR-BATCH-ECE-FILTERED + HR-BATCH-NO-PLACEHOLDER.
rules.exception: none. A surface that hides the filtering presents curated output as if it were the whole truth.

rules.title: a suggested test that cannot be proposed in one click is a catalogue, not a suggestion; "Proponer este test" always pre-loads the chosen hook into the creation flow
rules.trigger:
any operator-facing "suggested test" / recommended-creative surface with a "propose this test" action
(the /app tests-sugeridos cards; engines/ece/creative_batch consumed by the Guided Start).
rules.stop:
the propose action carries the EXACT chosen combination (hook_id,
hook_text, meat_format, cta_variant, awareness_level, proposed_budget_eur) into the creation flow -- the hook
lands pre-selected, its text shown as a preview, the budget pre-filled. The operator confirms or edits; they do
not start from zero. The deep-link is ADDITIVE: the same flow with no suggestion must behave byte-identically
(no regression to the unaided path).
rules.evidence:
2026-06-16 SL-119 -- "Proponer este test" linked to a bare /app/inicio-guiado/paso-1 with no hook
carried, forcing the operator to re-pick what the ECE had already recommended. Fixed with a sessionStorage
consume-once carry into the Guided Start creatives step (paso-4). Pairs with HR-TESTS-SUGERIDOS-TRANSPARENCY +
HR-BATCH-ECE-FILTERED.
rules.exception: none. A "propose" button that drops the operator into an empty flow is a dead-end disguised as an action.

rules.title: every completed experiment produces a non-empty learning_extracted that feeds the next operator's PKM; the outcomes are cross-operator and always anonymized (HR-D14)
rules.trigger: any experiment termination (kill_executor.evaluate_and_kill / scale_executor.evaluate_and_scale) and the PKM read (build_prior_knowledge_map) that consumes the outcomes.
rules.stop: (1) every kill/scale records an experiment_outcomes_compressed row whose learning_extracted is NEVER empty -- if the result has no obvious learning, the system documents WHY it was inconclusive (that is also learning); (2) the recorder is FAIL-OPEN -- a recorder error logs and the kill/scale stands (a kill that does not fire because the recorder raised is a far worse bug); the PKM outcome read is likewise fail-open (the pre-spend gate never breaks on an enrichment read); (3) the PKM read is cross-operator but ANONYMIZED -- it projects only hook_type/outcome_class/learning_extracted, NEVER tenant_email; outcomes surface at confidence=MEDIUM (above the YAML LOW prior, below the operator's own moat HIGH).
rules.evidence: 2026-06-17 SL-120 -- the ECE evaluated experiments PRE-spend from static memory but no result fed back POST-spend, so the same hook in the same niche was re-proposed with no memory of last time. Closed with experiment_outcomes_compressed + outcome_recorder + kill/scale hooks + PKM consumption. Pairs with HR-D14 (collective glass box) + HR-ECE-PRIOR-MANDATORY + HR-ECE-BLOCK-IS-VALUE.
rules.exception: none. A learning loop that can block a kill, or that exposes one operator's identity to another, is a net negative -- it must degrade silently-but-logged, never escalate.

rules.title: hook_type classification is deterministic (same hook -> same type), never an LLM at runtime; an unmatched hook is UNCLASSIFIED, never a forced guess
rules.trigger: any path that assigns a hook_type (ad_signal_ingester at ingest, outcome_recorder, the ECE proposal in confirm_launch_test) via engines/ece/hook_type_resolver.resolve_hook_type.
rules.stop: the resolver is pure + deterministic (direct match on a canonical type/alias -> id-embedded token run -> distinctive-template-keyword score). NO LLM call at runtime. A weak, ambiguous, or tied signal returns UNCLASSIFIED. No type is ever emitted outside loader.get_canonical_hook_types() (the hooks_library.yaml category keys + legacy aliases). A free-text classifier that "always returns something" is forbidden -- the bar for a confident type is high and the default is UNCLASSIFIED.
rules.evidence: 2026-06-17 SL-121 -- market_signals/ad_performance_signals stored hook_type="unclassified" for every ad, starving the PKM/WPR/OGD/CRE of the type signal. Closed with a deterministic resolver wired at ingest + recorder + ECE proposal. Pairs with HR-TASTE-DETERMINISTIC + HR-NICHE-01 (the niche classifier's same honest-unclassified doctrine).
rules.exception: none. A forced classification that is wrong is strictly worse than UNCLASSIFIED because it poisons shared learning.

rules.title: concentration of the same hook_type in a niche is wasted capital; the ECE recommends hook_type diversity as a condition of valid learning (a COMPRESS, never a retroactive kill)
rules.trigger: the ECE compression ladder (engines/ece/compression_engine.decide_compression) when an operator proposes a test whose hook_type they already run >= _DIVERSIFICATION_CAP times active in the niche.
rules.stop: the proposal is COMPRESSed with reason DIVERSIFICATION, redirecting the operator to a different archetype. It is advisory intelligence, not a hard block, and it NEVER kills or alters a live ad (it only shapes the NEW proposal). It is not a euro saving (no budget cut), so expected_savings_eur/compression_ratio stay zero (HR-VAULT-PRIOR: never invent a saving). A lower ladder rung (a known-failure BLOCK, a proven-winner INFER, a proxy shortcut) always precedes it. It fires only for a real classified hook_type, never for UNCLASSIFIED.
rules.evidence: 2026-06-17 SL-121 -- once hook_type was classified (HR-HOOK-TYPE-DETERMINISTIC), the ECE could finally see same-type concentration. Pairs with HR-ECE-BLOCK-IS-VALUE + HR-ECE-LEARNING-LOOP.
rules.exception: the Owner may raise/lower _DIVERSIFICATION_CAP per the niche's maturity; the gate itself (diversity = learning) is doctrine.

rules.title: a winning pattern enters the vault at HIGH confidence ONLY when 3+ DISTINCT operators have independently confirmed it; one operator (however successful) is never HIGH
rules.trigger: the Winner Pattern Registry assigns a confidence tier to a (hook_type, awareness_level) winning pattern (engines/cbin/winner_pattern_registry.classify_confidence / extract_winning_patterns), and that pattern feeds the PKM as its top-confidence source (prior_knowledge_map: SOURCE_WPR outranks moat + vault prior).
rules.stop: confidence = HIGH iff n_independent >= 3 where n_independent = COUNT(DISTINCT tenant_email) computed IN SQL (the identity never leaves the database -- HR-D14); MEDIUM iff n_winners >= 2; else LOW. Two winners from the SAME operator is MEDIUM, never HIGH (one successful operator is not independent replication). The registry is derived LIVE from experiment_outcomes_compressed (ZERO-DDL, no duplicated YAML/table that would drift -- ECE_FUSED DL-F2 + CBIN Registro 1). An UNCLASSIFIED hook never becomes a pattern. No invented pattern: an empty niche returns [] (HR-VAULT-PRIOR).
rules.evidence: 2026-06-17 SL-122 -- with hook_type classified (SL-121) + the Learning Loop feeding outcomes (SL-120), the system could finally extract specific patterns ("IDENTITY in fitness at ROAS 2.8x with 5 confirmations") and promote the replicated ones to the PKM's HIGH tier. Pairs with HR-D14 (anonymity), HR-VAULT-PRIOR (no invented priors), HR-ECE-LEARNING-LOOP.
rules.exception: the Owner may raise the independence threshold for a higher bar; lowering it below 3 distinct operators is forbidden (it re-admits unreplicated noise as HIGH).

rules.title: the FGR detects and registers failure patterns no a-priori corpus anticipated; an EMERGENT pattern was discovered by real operators paying with capital, and 2 independent confirmations is enough to act on it
rules.trigger: the Failure Genome Registry classifies a (hook_type, awareness_level, failure_mode) genome from LOSER/KILLED_EARLY outcomes (engines/cbin/failure_genome_registry: _classify_mode + classify_failure_confidence), feeding the PKM's known_failures + the ECE BLOCK.
rules.stop: ``emergent=True`` iff the failure_mode is in neither a-priori source; it is NOT discarded for being undocumented -- a 2-independent-operator EMERGENT pattern is MEDIUM (actionable: prevent at MEDIUM, do not wait for HIGH to block). Operational kill-modes are NOT force-mapped onto FP ids (HR-VAULT-PRIOR -- they are a different taxonomy); ``mapped_fp`` is set only when the mode literally is a catalog FP id. confidence = HIGH at 3+ independent operators, MEDIUM at 2, LOW at 1; n_confirmations = COUNT(DISTINCT tenant_email) in SQL (HR-D14). Derived live from experiment_outcomes_compressed (ZERO-DDL; gce_failure_pattern_emergent does not exist and is not a dependency).
rules.evidence: 2026-06-17 SL-123 -- the SL-120 Learning Loop records operational kill-modes (cpa_above_target, roas_below_floor, ...), a DIFFERENT taxonomy from the strategic FP-1..FP-10 business corpus, so most empirical failures surface as EMERGENT -- which is precisely the FGR's value. Pairs with HR-WPR-CONFIDENCE-HONEST (the symmetric winner registry), HR-D14, HR-VAULT-PRIOR.
rules.exception: the Owner may raise the act-on threshold; treating a 2-independent EMERGENT pattern as noise (ignoring it because it is absent from the corpus) is forbidden.

rules.title: the Failure Genome Registry and the Winner Pattern Registry are complementary, not redundant: the WPR says what works, the FGR says what fails, and the PKM with both is market intelligence, not a filter
rules.trigger: any change to the ECE pre-spend gate (build_prior_knowledge_map, decide_compression) that touches the winner OR failure registries.
rules.stop: keep the two registries symmetric and independent -- the WPR promotes winners to known_winning_patterns (SOURCE_WPR, outranks moat/vault), the FGR promotes failures to known_failures (FGR HIGH -> L0 BLOCK with avg_cpa_at_death, MEDIUM -> advisory). Neither subsumes the other; both derive live from experiment_outcomes_compressed; both gate HIGH on 3+ independent operators. KNOWN EDGE (documented, not a bug): L0 (FGR BLOCK) precedes L1 (WPR INFER), so a hook that is BOTH a 3-operator winner and a 3-operator failure in one niche is blocked conservatively -- a self-contradictory empirical state where caution is correct.
rules.evidence: 2026-06-17 SL-123 -- the FGR was built as the WPR's mirror (same extract -> tier -> PKM -> endpoint shape). Pairs with HR-WPR-CONFIDENCE-HONEST, HR-FGR-EMERGENT-DETECTION.
rules.exception: none -- the symmetry is doctrine.

rules.title: the Hook Genome's fitness_score is DERIVED and never fabricated; None when no empirical data exists, and QUARANTINE (not a number) when a hook is both a HIGH winner and a HIGH failure
rules.trigger: the Hook Genome Registry derives a fitness_score for a (hook_type, niche) from its 4 layers (engines/cbin/hook_genome_registry.compute_fitness / build_hook_genome / build_genome_index), feeding the /cbin/hook-genome endpoints + the creative-batch ranking.
rules.stop: fitness_score is None (status UNKNOWN) when NO layer carries data (no ego_score AND no WPR/FGR >= MEDIUM); it is None (status QUARANTINE) when the hook is BOTH a HIGH WPR winner and a HIGH FGR failure (a contradiction the registry refuses to score with a number). The HGR is a JOIN of 4 EXISTING sources (static hooks_library YAML + ego_score CPM aggregate + WPR + FGR) keyed by hook_type -- ZERO-DDL (CBIN Registro 4 + DL-F2), never a new table. The genome-fitness enrichment is FAIL-OPEN everywhere it is consumed (the creative batch / menu must rank without it if a source read fails). HR-D14: every source is already anonymized (CPM has no operator column; WPR/FGR carry counts).
rules.evidence: 2026-06-17 SL-125 -- with the WPR (SL-122) + FGR (SL-123) both live, the HGR could JOIN static + performance + winning + losing into one Hook Genome per archetype. Pairs with HR-WPR-CONFIDENCE-HONEST, HR-FGR-EMERGENT-DETECTION, HR-VAULT-PRIOR.
rules.exception: none -- a fabricated fitness number poisons the operator's read of which hooks are proven vs unknown.

rules.title: the Creative DNA Registry registers combinations that WIN TOGETHER, not combinations that merely co-occur; a trio equally frequent in winners and losers is NOISE and is never registered, however frequent
rules.trigger: the Creative DNA Registry derives a co-occurrence-scored combination from outcomes (engines/cbin/creative_dna_registry: compute_co_occurrence / build_creative_dna / extract_creative_dna), feeding the creative-batch ranking, the HGR best_creative_dna 5th layer, and GET /cbin/creative-dna/{niche}.
rules.stop: a trio is registered as DNA ONLY when (1) hook_type is classified (SL-121), (2) n_winners >= min_winners, AND (3) co_occurrence_score is strictly above the noise floor (more concentrated in winners than losers). confidence is HIGH at 3+ INDEPENDENT operators, MEDIUM at 2, LOW at 1 (the WPR replication ladder). ZERO-DDL: the trio aggregate is a read-time GROUP BY over experiment_outcomes_compressed (CBIN Registro 2 + DL-F2), one query with CASE conditional aggregation splitting the winner leg from the loser leg, never a new table. The CDR is FAIL-OPEN everywhere consumed (the batch / HGR rank without it if the read fails). HR-D14: n_confirmations is COUNT(DISTINCT tenant_email) over WINNING rows, computed in SQL -- the email never leaves the database. The CDR does NOT alter the HGR fitness_score (additive 'so use it like this' annotation only).
rules.evidence: 2026-06-17 SL-126 -- with the HGR (SL-125) scoring hooks in isolation, the CDR rose a level: a hook that wins alone can still fail wrapped in the wrong meat_format/awareness, so the CDR captures which COMBINATIONS win together. Substrate already present (meat_format + awareness_level columns on experiment_outcomes_compressed since SL-120) -> pure JOIN, Scenario A (Zero-DDL). Pairs with HR-WPR-CONFIDENCE-HONEST, HR-FGR-EMERGENT-DETECTION, HR-HOOK-GENOME-HONEST, HR-D14, HR-VAULT-PRIOR.
rules.exception: the Owner may raise the noise floor or the independence threshold for a higher bar; registering a trio whose co_occurrence_score is at/below the floor (equal win/lose concentration) as DNA is forbidden -- that re-admits noise as signal.

rules.title: the Offer DNA Registry NEVER exposes any operator exact price; prices are grouped into bands (LOW/MID/HIGH) at the capture seam, BEFORE anything is stored, and the exact value is discarded
rules.trigger: any code that captures or surfaces an offer structure for the ODR (engines/ece/offer_snapshot.build_offer_snapshot / price_band; engines/cbin/offer_dna_registry; the /cbin/offer-dna endpoint; the FWE gate / creative-batch offer advisory).
rules.stop: the offer signature stored in learning_extracted.offer and every ODR read carries price_BAND only -- never an exact price, an AOV figure tied to an operator, or any field that could reconstruct a price. ZERO-DDL: the anonymized snapshot rides the EXISTING learning_extracted JSON (no new column), read live like the FGR reads failure_mode. HR-D14: n_confirmations is COUNT(DISTINCT tenant_email) over winning rows, computed in SQL. The ODR reads honestly-empty until offer-bearing outcomes accrue (no fabricated offer DNA -- HR-VAULT-PRIOR).
rules.evidence: 2026-06-17 SL-129 -- the CDR (SL-126) captured creative DNA; the ODR rose a level to offer DNA (price band + guarantee + shipping + bundle + urgency). The anchor found the offer structure is persisted NOWHERE joinable to outcomes (offer_builder generates it transiently; FirstWinProfile/OperatorProfile carry no offer fields), so the ODR is an offer-capture FOUNDATION (Owner-approved): the anonymized snapshot rides learning_extracted via a recorder seam + executor wiring, and the registry derives DNA forward. Pairs with HR-CDR-CO-OCCURRENCE-HONEST, HR-D14, HR-VAULT-PRIOR, HR-ODR-ADVISORY-NOT-BLOCK.
rules.exception: none -- exposing an exact price cross-operator is forbidden without exception. The Owner may narrow the bands further but never widen them to reveal a price.

rules.title: the ODR offer advisory in the FWE gate is INFORMATION, never a block; the operator may launch with any offer structure, and the system only tells them what the ODR knows
rules.trigger: the FWE pre-market gate (confirm_launch_test) or the creative-batch endpoint attaches an ODR-derived offer advisory/recommendation (api/fwe_router: offer_advisory / offer_recommendation).
rules.stop: the advisory is a RESPONSE FIELD only -- it never changes plan_status, never raises a 409, never alters the approved budget. Computed FAIL-OPEN (an ODR read failure leaves the advisory None and the launch unaffected) and only populated when the ODR has a HIGH-confidence (3+ independent operators) offer pattern for the niche. None otherwise -- no advisory invented from thin data.
rules.evidence: 2026-06-17 SL-129 -- the operator own offer is not captured at launch (the substrate gap the foundation addresses forward), so the advisory presents the niche winning offer DNA as a recommendation, not an operator-specific diff. Pairs with HR-ODR-PRICE-PRIVACY, HR-VALIDATION-GATE, HR-ECE-BLOCK-IS-VALUE.
rules.exception: none -- the ODR advisory must never become a launch gate. (Distinct from the ECE pre-spend BLOCK and the pre-market validation gate, which ARE blocks by separate sealed design.)

rules.title: the Learning Velocity Score reports UNKNOWN for a new system because that IS the correct state; a velocity reported without data is a fabrication, and a dimension with no real clock is an honest None, never an invented number
rules.trigger: any code that computes or surfaces a learning-velocity number (engines/gce/learning_velocity: calculate_lvs / compounding_velocity_days / data_sufficiency_for / lvs_status_for / build_velocity_karma_report; GET /gce/learning-velocity/{niche}; the GCE_LIVE_STATUS Naif metric).
rules.stop: only the COMPOUNDING dimension is real -- it derives from experiment_outcomes_compressed.created_at (the one durable dated signal): the span first_seen->last_seen over which a winning pattern gathered N distinct operators. EXTRACTION and PROMOTION velocity are STRUCTURAL None (not missing-data None): the WPR/FGR registries and the KnowledgeAsset layer are derived-live (Zero-DDL), so a learning exists the instant its outcome row lands and an asset has no persisted "time it reached L4" -- there is no latency to measure, and the None is surfaced as structural_none=("extraction","promotion") so the Owner knows it is unmeasurable here, not just absent. data_sufficiency: INSUFFICIENT until a pattern reaches a 2nd independent operator (LIMITED), SUFFICIENT at the WPR HIGH threshold (3 independent). UNKNOWN/INSUFFICIENT carries a message that NAMES the unblocker (when the next operator's cycle produces the first datapoint). HR-D14: the timespan read counts operators in SQL (COUNT DISTINCT tenant_email), never returns identity. The owner velocity digest returns None (suppressed) when no niche is measurable -- an empty report is noise.
rules.evidence: 2026-06-18 SL-132 -- the GCE's 5 substrate submotors (ECE/WPR/FGR/CBLR/KC) were all live and derived-live; the LVS rose a level to MEASURE the chain's velocity. The anchor found the prompt's premise (a persisted upgrade_history / asset created_at to subtract for promotion velocity, and a batch extraction step with latency) does NOT exist in the codebase -- the architecture is derived-live, so two of the three dimensions are unmeasurable by construction. Rather than fabricate them, the LVS reports them as structural None and delivers the one real dimension. Pairs with HR-WPR-CONFIDENCE-HONEST, HR-VAULT-PRIOR, HR-D14, HR-KC-COMPOUND-HONEST, HR-GCE-FLYWHEEL-CLOSED.
rules.exception: none -- fabricating a velocity number to avoid showing UNKNOWN, or presenting a structurally-None dimension as a real latency, is forbidden. A dashboard that lies is worse than one that honestly says "not yet". (A future audit table could light up extraction/promotion; until it exists, they stay structural None.)

rules.title: with the LVS live the GCE has 6/6 submotors operational; the flywheel is closed (each cycle feeds the next) and the system can DEMONSTRATE it compounds with real data the moment the first operator closes the first cross-operator cycle
rules.trigger: declaring the GCE complete or documenting its status (vault/cuae/gce/GCE_LIVE_STATUS.md), or any change to the submotor set / the flywheel wiring (engines/gce/*, the PKM source list in prior_knowledge_map, the ECE pre-spend read).
rules.stop: the 6 submotors are LVS (SL-132) + ECE (SL-116) + WPR (SL-122) + FGR (SL-123) + CBLR (SL-126) + KC (SL-128->129). All derived-live / Zero-DDL except the CBLR's persisted route objects. GCE_LIVE_STATUS.md MUST document all 6 with their SL numbers and the closed flywheel; the Naif compounding metric MUST state the honest current state (UNKNOWN -- system new), the unblocker (the 2nd independent operator's cycle), and the benchmark (compounding velocity, target < ~14d). The LVS does not DRIVE the flywheel -- it is the instrument that proves it turns.
rules.evidence: 2026-06-18 SL-132 -- the LVS was the only remaining GCE submotor (GCE_LIVE_STATUS 5/6). With it live the loop is closed and instrumented. The anchor found a prior session had already built most of the calculator + endpoint + tests (uncommitted in the worktree); this seal extends rather than duplicates (the STOP-if-partial path). Pairs with HR-LVS-UNKNOWN-IS-HONEST, HR-KC-COMPOUND-HONEST, HR-CBIN-COMPLETE.
rules.exception: none -- claiming the GCE compounds without the LVS instrument in place, or declaring 6/6 while a submotor is only partially wired, is forbidden. The proof is the measured datapoint, not the architecture diagram.

rules.title: the Profit Reinvestment Router routes only profit it can VERIFY; profit that cannot be computed is None (never 0, never invented), and an option whose supporting data is absent is viable=False with an honest reason, never a fabricated projection
rules.trigger: any code that computes or surfaces an operator's reinvestable profit or ranks reinvestment options (engines/revenue_accel/reinvestment_router: _available_profit / decide_reinvestment / evaluate_reinvestment_options / propose_reinvestment; GET /api/v1/rae/reinvestment-options; the karma reinvestment_proposed message; the /app/capital ReinvestmentOptionsPanel).
rules.stop: available_profit_eur is derived = max(0, verified_gross_last_30d - declared_ad_spend_last_30d - verified_gross * declared_product_cost_pct), refund-reserve-adjusted via aio.refund_reserve.reserve_breakdown (reinvestable = verified_profit - refund_reserve). It is None (D15: None != 0) when there is no operator profile, no verified Shopify gross, or either declared cost leg is absent -- a net profit cannot be honestly computed without its costs. When None -> only VAULT_RESERVE is viable and is the recommendation; the karma proposal is suppressed. The deployable figure is clamped to min(reinvestable, spendable_after_reserve) so the reserve floor is inviolable (RAL-3). The router NEVER defaults to scaling (RAL-4): SCALE_WINNER is recommended only when a winner clears the scale ROAS bar AND the niche is not saturated AND the D07 momentum cap is not tripped; otherwise it diversifies (deepen the niche / expand to a good adjacent) or reserves. The PRODUCTION option is always viable=False with an honest reason (the CEE that would measure production efficiency does not exist yet) -- an honest not-evaluable, never a projection. Routing is deterministic + explainable (RAL-5): same inputs -> same plan, each option carries its own reasoning. Zero-DDL: nothing is persisted; the option set + reasoning ride KarmaEvent.payload as the audit trail; the endpoint is operator-gated + anonymized (no tenant identity in the response).
rules.evidence: 2026-06-18 SL-133 -- the GCE was complete (6/6) and could learn/compress/compose, but when a winner generated verified profit nothing decided what to DO with it (kill_scale_router is binary, capital_banding only permits/blocks). The PRR (RAE gap P0) closes that loop. The anchor found the integration-anchor's v1 plan called for a new rae_reinvestment_decision table, but the Owner's prompt mandated Zero-DDL -> resolved derived-live + audit-via-KarmaEvent.payload (the established GCE/CBIN pattern). Pairs with HR-PRR-FAIL-OPEN, HR-MIGRATION-NO-AUTOESCALE, HR-D14, HR-VAULT-PRIOR, HR-GCE-FLYWHEEL-CLOSED.
rules.exception: none -- showing a reinvestable-profit number the system cannot verify, presenting PRODUCTION as evaluable without the CEE, or defaulting to scale because it is the easy option, is forbidden. A fabricated projection could make the operator scale past what their capital can sustain.

rules.title: the Profit Reinvestment Router never blocks the release of verified profit and never commits capital; a PRR failure is a loss of GUIDANCE, never a loss of capital
rules.trigger: any hook that calls the PRR from a capital/profit path (workers/tasks/sync_shopify_revenue propose_reinvestment hook; the karma emit of reinvestment_proposed; the /app/capital panel fetch).
rules.stop: propose_reinvestment wraps everything in try/except returning None -- a failure logs and the caller proceeds (the sync hook runs AFTER the verified-gross update + graduation are already computed, so a PRR error cannot un-verify profit). v1 emits notify-only (action_runner=None): even at ACT level no runner executes, so the PRR NEVER commits capital -- it lands prepared for the operator to confirm (tier RECOMMEND, D06). The endpoint and panel are ADVISORY: viewing an option never executes it. The frontend panel degrades to a non-blocking "no disponible" card on any fetch error.
rules.evidence: 2026-06-18 SL-133 -- the PRR is portfolio-level guidance bolted onto the money path; the kill/scale executors already commit capital under their own gates, and the PRR must add decision value without ever becoming a new failure mode for capital. Pairs with HR-PRR-NO-FABRICATED-PROJECTION, HR-ODR-ADVISORY-NOT-BLOCK, HR-KARMA-AUTONOMY.
rules.exception: none -- wiring the PRR so that a proposal failure blocks the revenue sync, or so that an ACT auto-executes a reinvestment in v1, is forbidden.

rules.title: the Spend Rate Engine observes and notifies, NEVER acts; it never pauses, scales or kills an experiment -- the kill stays the kill_executor's job (its CPA thresholds are proven + sealed), the scale the scale_executor's
rules.trigger: any code in engines/revenue_accel/spend_rate (evaluate_burn_rate / analyze_spend_rate / check_spend_rates / _emit_spend_rate_warning), the spend_rate_warning karma event/message, GET /api/v1/rae/spend-rate, or the SRE hook in workers/tasks/ad_spend_pull._drain.
rules.stop: the SRE reads (active executions + observed spend/ROAS + niche roas_breakeven + capital_banding spendable-after-reserve) and EMITS a karma spend_rate_warning -- nothing else. It NEVER mutates an experiment's status, never calls kill_executor/scale_executor, never pauses a campaign. burn_rate_status = actual/expected ROAS ratio (>=1x SUSTAINABLE, [0.6,1x) ELEVATED, <0.6x CRITICAL); UNKNOWN (honest, never fabricated) when days_of_data < 3 OR ROAS not yet measured OR no positive spend; days_until_floor is None when UNKNOWN (no invented runway). The warning fires only on ELEVATED/CRITICAL, throttled to at most one per EXPERIMENT per 24h (keyed on payload.experiment_id, so a sibling experiment of the same operator is never suppressed). spend_rate_warning is NOT in CAPITAL_EVENT_TYPES and NOT in KARMA_EVENT_TYPES (a protective burn notice, not a capital action nor a UI toggle). check_spend_rates is fail-open per experiment and runs inside the already-guarded ad_spend_pull drain, so a SRE failure never breaks the spend tick.
rules.evidence: 2026-06-18 SL-136 -- with the GCE 6/6 and the PRR (RAE gap P0) live, the RAE's next pure-reader submotor was needed: without a burn-rate observer the operator could run out of capital before the ECE/kill_executor detects the experiment is a loser (the kill looks at accumulated CPA, after the fact). NAMING NOTE: the RAE corpus's "SRE" is the Scale Readiness Engine (a pre-scale checklist); THIS is the Spend Rate Engine the Owner's prompt specified (a burn-rate monitor) -- distinct submotor, same acronym, the Owner spec governs (PR-DECISIONS). Pairs with HR-PRR-FAIL-OPEN, HR-ODR-ADVISORY-NOT-BLOCK, HR-MSR-SHIFT-HONEST, HR-KARMA-AUTONOMY.
rules.exception: none -- wiring the SRE to pause, cancel or auto-kill an experiment, or to move the operator's capital without their last approval, is forbidden. A SRE that acts violates the system's core philosophy: the operator's capital always requires their own last word to move. The kill belongs to the kill_executor's sealed CPA thresholds; the SRE is the early warning, not the trigger.

rules.title: the Revenue Bottleneck Detector diagnoses from real data or it says INSUFFICIENT_DATA; never a fabricated diagnosis to look more useful, and estimated_revenue_unlock_eur is None when it is not honestly calculable -- a wrong diagnosis sends the operator to fix the wrong problem
rules.trigger: any code in engines/revenue_accel/revenue_bottleneck (rank_bottlenecks / detect_revenue_bottleneck / report_bottleneck / check_revenue_bottlenecks), experiment_outcome_repo.get_operator_outcome_stats, the revenue_bottleneck_report karma event/message, GET /api/v1/rae/revenue-bottleneck, the RevenueBottleneckPanel surface, or the RBD hook in workers/tasks/ad_spend_pull._drain.
rules.stop: the RBD reads (FirstWinProfile band + spendable-after-reserve, active ad_executions, the operator's OWN 30-day outcome stats, the MSR niche shift severity) and EMITS a weekly karma revenue_bottleneck_report -- nothing else. It NEVER spends, scales, pauses or kills (DIAGNOSE-ONLY; zero mutation paths -- verified by G2 exhaustion). Six factors: CAPITAL_FLOOR + NO_ACTIVE_TEST are BLOCKING (revenue cannot grow at all), NICHE_SATURATION + WINNER_RATE are SIGNIFICANT (revenue materially constrained), HOOK_DIVERSITY is MINOR (only learning is slowed); primary_bottleneck = the highest-severity factor (deterministic tie-break), recommended_action names the resolving system (PRR/MSR/FGR/HGR). estimated_revenue_unlock_eur is ALWAYS None in v1 (a revenue-unlock projection needs the CEE which does not exist; the capital GAP lives in the factor detail, a forward REVENUE number is never invented). WINNER_RATE needs >= 3 tests (noise floor); INSUFFICIENT_DATA when there is no operator profile and NEVER emits a report (the STOP). revenue_bottleneck_report is NOT in CAPITAL_EVENT_TYPES and NOT in KARMA_EVENT_TYPES; throttled to at most one per TENANT per 7 days (per-operator, unlike the SRE's per-experiment -- a strategic diagnosis is operator-level). check_revenue_bottlenecks is fail-open per operator inside the already-guarded ad_spend_pull drain; the MSR read is degraded-not-fatal. HR-D14: tenant_email is only a WHERE filter in get_operator_outcome_stats (the operator's OWN data), never selected -- no other operator's identity leaks. Zero-DDL (derived-live; audit rides KarmaEvent.payload).
rules.evidence: 2026-06-18 SL-138 -- with the PRR (capital routing) and SRE (burn alert) live, the RAE lacked a STRATEGIC reader: an operator with healthy ROAS can still have revenue capped by no capital to scale, a saturating niche, a low winner rate, or one-dimensional hooks, and nothing told them which to fix first. The RBD is the diagnosis layer; like the PRR/SRE it is a pure reader that never moves capital. Pairs with HR-SRE-OBSERVE-NOT-ACT, HR-PRR-FAIL-OPEN, HR-PRR-NO-FABRICATED-PROJECTION, HR-MSR-SHIFT-HONEST.
rules.exception: none -- wiring the RBD to act on its diagnosis (pause/scale/kill/spend), fabricating estimated_revenue_unlock_eur, or emitting a diagnosis for an operator the system cannot honestly diagnose (INSUFFICIENT_DATA) is forbidden. A diagnosis without data is fabrication; a wrong diagnosis is worse than none -- it sends the operator to fix the wrong problem while the real bottleneck bleeds revenue.

rules.title: the Next Best Growth Action Engine produces EXACTLY ONE recommendation; the operator never has to choose between three Karma events, and an URGENT signal (SRE CRITICAL / RBD BLOCKING) can never be masked by a HIGH opportunity (PRR scale) -- scaling with a critical burn or no capital is scaling toward bankruptcy
rules.trigger: any code in engines/revenue_accel/next_best_action (synthesize_growth_action / get_next_best_action / report_growth_action / check_growth_actions), the growth_action_recommended karma event/message, GET /api/v1/rae/next-action, the NextBestActionPanel surface, or the NBGAE hook in workers/tasks/ad_spend_pull._drain.
rules.stop: get_next_best_action reads the three submotors (PRR evaluate_reinvestment_options + SRE analyze_spend_rate + RBD detect_revenue_bottleneck, each fail-soft -- a degraded read contributes an honest default, never breaks the synthesis) and synthesize_growth_action folds them into EXACTLY ONE GrowthAction by a deterministic precedence ladder: URGENT [SRE CRITICAL -> REDUCE_BURN; RBD CAPITAL_FLOOR -> RESOLVE_CAPITAL_FLOOR; RBD NO_ACTIVE_TEST -> LAUNCH_FIRST_TEST] > INSUFFICIENT_DATA (no profile) > HIGH [PRR verified+SCALE_WINNER -> SCALE_WINNER; RBD NICHE_SATURATION -> DIVERSIFY_NICHE; RBD WINNER_RATE -> DIVERSIFY_HOOKS] > MEDIUM [RBD HOOK_DIVERSITY -> DIVERSIFY_HOOKS; else -> MAINTAIN_AND_MONITOR]. PRR contributes only its flagship SCALE_WINNER to the selection (its NEW_TEST/NEW_NICHE/VAULT_RESERVE enrich supporting_data and stay in the PRR panel) so exactly one action is ever primary. cape_gap is ALWAYS True in v1 (the CEE does not exist, so the conversion dimension is missing -- the operator is told the diagnosis will improve, never a hidden limitation). The growth_action_recommended karma event emits ONLY when the action_id CHANGED vs the operator's last digest AND at most once per 24h per tenant (no spam); INSUFFICIENT_DATA NEVER emits (its banner lives in /app, not Karma). growth_action_recommended is NOT in CAPITAL_EVENT_TYPES and NOT in KARMA_EVENT_TYPES. RECOMMEND-ONLY: the NBGAE never spends/scales/pauses/kills (verified by G2 exhaustion); the directive headline is for clarity, the operator executes. Zero-DDL (derived-live over the three submotors; audit rides KarmaEvent.payload). FE CTA buttons point only to verified routes (/app/campanas, /app/inicio-guiado/paso-1) or the in-page #reinvest anchor -- never a fabricated route.
rules.evidence: 2026-06-19 SL-139 -- the RAE's three readers (PRR/SRE/RBD) each spoke separately; without synthesis the operator carries the integration burden and may optimize a sub-metric (scale a winner) while the real problem (a critical burn) bleeds them out. The NBGAE is the synthesizer that makes the RAE actionable as ONE voice. CAPE remains blocked by the CEE; NBGAE v1 ships without the conversion dimension (cape_gap=True, honest), valuable now and upgradeable when the CEE lands. Pairs with HR-RBD-DIAGNOSIS-HONEST, HR-SRE-OBSERVE-NOT-ACT, HR-PRR-FAIL-OPEN, HR-PRR-NO-FABRICATED-PROJECTION.
rules.exception: none -- wiring the NBGAE to produce more than one action, to act on its recommendation (spend/scale/pause/kill), to let a HIGH opportunity mask an URGENT signal, to emit a Karma event for INSUFFICIENT_DATA or when nothing changed, or to link a CTA to a nonexistent route is forbidden. The operator's capital always requires their own last word to move.

rules.title: the CEE conversion rate is an ESTIMATE derived from real traffic (clicks->purchase), never a direct landing-page measurement; a DERIVED 3.6% CVR is a different thing from a MEASURED 3.6%, and the CAPE that consumes it carries the estimated label downstream -- a projection that mixes DERIVED with MEASURED unlabelled has compromised integrity
rules.trigger: any code in engines/revenue_accel/conversion_estimator (derive_cvr / estimate_conversion_rate / _niche_cr_midpoint), engines/revenue_accel/compound_advantage (project_compound_advantage / calculate_compound_advantage), AdSpendRepository.clicks_sum_by_campaign, the cape_gap resolution in next_best_action.get_next_best_action, or GET /api/v1/rae/compound-advantage + the CompoundAdvantagePanel surface.
rules.stop: the CEE returns a ConversionEstimate whose method+confidence are honest -- DERIVED_FROM_TRAFFIC (operator conversions/clicks, MEDIUM -- the conversions are a time-window attribution not a pixel, so NEVER HIGH), NICHE_PRIOR (cr_landing_pct midpoint/100, ESTIMATED), or UNKNOWN (cvr None, never fabricated). The rejected CPA/AOV formula is absent from the code. CAPE blends only the dimensions whose data is real (conversion_contribution needs BOTH operator + niche CVR; hook/market need a diagnosable RBD); a missing dimension contributes None, never a number; current_efficiency_score falls to a neutral 0.5 baseline when no dimension has data (never a fabricated 0 or 1). projected_efficiency_90d is None unless cape_confidence == MEDIUM (>= 5 real conversion cycles -- the STOP); cape_confidence ladder ESTIMATED(0) < LOW(1-4) < MEDIUM(5+) by cee_data_points. The NBGAE resolves cape_gap = (cape_confidence != ESTIMATED ? False : True) via dataclasses.replace, fail-soft (a CAPE failure leaves cape_gap True). Both engines are READ-ONLY (zero mutation paths, G2 exhaustion); both async gatherers fail-soft. Zero-DDL (clicks_sum_by_campaign is a pure SELECT over the existing clicks column; nothing persisted).
rules.evidence: 2026-06-20 SL-140 -- CAPE was the last RAE engine, blocked by a non-existent CEE. The CEE v1 unblocks it by deriving the conversion dimension from clicks+conversions that already exist (no Shopify-traffic integration), honestly labelled DERIVED/ESTIMATED so CAPE inherits the estimate's confidence ceiling. The full CEE (direct landing-page traffic) and a higher-confidence CAPE remain future work; v1 is honest about being an estimate. Pairs with HR-NBGAE-ONE-ACTION, HR-RBD-DIAGNOSIS-HONEST, HR-MSR-SHIFT-HONEST, HR-PRR-NO-FABRICATED-PROJECTION.
rules.exception: none -- presenting a DERIVED CVR as a measured one, labelling the CEE estimate HIGH, using the CPA/AOV (=ROAS) formula as a conversion rate, CAPE projecting beyond what the cycles support, or fabricating a per-dimension contribution / efficiency score is forbidden. A projection built on a mislabelled conversion metric misleads the operator about where their real advantage is.

rules.title: the Winner Portability Analyzer NEVER declares a market PORTABLE without empirical base; every dimension that lacks real data is labelled ESTIMATED, and a 0.79 ESTIMATED is a different thing from a 0.79 MEDIUM -- the operator is told which; a blocking dimension (no shared language) forces NOT_PORTABLE and that block is correct, never softened to make the recommendation nicer
rules.trigger: any code that scores market portability or sequences market expansion (engines/cmss/mee/winner_portability: score_portability / the 5 _*_dim helpers / _overall; engines/cmss/mee/sequential_launcher: recommend_next_market; engines/cmss/mee/expansion_insight: maybe_propose_expansion; GET /cmss/market-expansion/{niche}; the market_expansion_ready Karma message).
rules.stop: each of the 5 dimensions (economic, linguistic, competitive, cultural, regulatory) carries its OWN confidence (HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW/ESTIMATED). ESTIMATED is mandatory where no real per-market datum exists: REGULATORY is ALWAYS ESTIMATED (no per-market regulation data -- never declares a product permitted, always "verifica regulaciones locales"); CULTURAL is ALWAYS ESTIMATED (the outcome corpus is not segmented by market); ECONOMIC is ESTIMATED for a null-economics market (latam/usa) and never borrows the canonical (europe) number. LINGUISTIC is deterministic (shared language -> HIGH; no overlap -> a BLOCKING dimension). overall_confidence is the WEAKEST link (the report is only as trustworthy as its least-supported dimension). overall_verdict is PORTABLE only at mean score >= 0.7 AND no blocking dimension below the floor; a blocking dimension forces NOT_PORTABLE regardless of the mean. The MSR (SL-131) feeds competitive and the WPR (SL-122) feeds cultural -- both FAIL-OPEN (a read failure degrades the dimension to ESTIMATED, never breaks the report). The market_expansion_ready Karma event is ADVISORY (action_runner=None): it surfaces opportunity, commits no capital, and never migrates (the MME owns migration). HR-MARKET-AXIS-DEFAULT-EUROPE: the operator's source_market defaults to europe, never fabricated.
rules.evidence: 2026-06-18 SL-135 -- with GAP-1 (per-operator source_market) and the MME (SL-134, one-migration) live, the MEE rose a level to the PORTFOLIO: which markets to expand into, ranked by portability + capital readiness. The anchor found the work already built (uncommitted in the tuax-mme worktree); a sibling pane committed it to main (d13be77) during the finish-and-ship approval. Two of the five dimensions are structurally ESTIMATED (no per-market regulation / market-segmented corpus exists) -- rather than fabricate them, the WPA labels them and lets the weakest-link confidence carry the honesty. Pairs with HR-MARKET-AXIS-DEFAULT-EUROPE, HR-MSR-SHIFT-HONEST, HR-WPR-CONFIDENCE-HONEST, HR-VAULT-PRIOR, HR-D14.
rules.exception: none -- softening a blocking linguistic verdict, presenting an ESTIMATED score as a measured one, or declaring PORTABLE below the empirical bar to make a recommendation more appealing is forbidden. An honest LOW_PORTABILITY protects the operator's capital; a fabricated PORTABLE spends it on a market the data never supported.

rules.title: the post-waitlist nurture emails never promise a seat, a timeline, or revenue; they confirm the signup and explain the real mechanism, and a "results" milestone renders ONLY the Owner's real numbers, never a fabricated figure
rules.trigger: any code that composes or broadcasts a waitlist nurture email (engines/waitlist/emails: joined_email / milestone_first_test_email / milestone_first_results_email; the POST /api/v1/admin/waitlist/broadcast endpoint; the BroadcastMilestone admin control).
rules.stop: EMAIL 1 (joined) confirms the signup with NO revenue/timeline/seat promise. EMAIL 2 (first_test milestone) announces that the first operator is running a REAL test -- it carries NO fabricated metric (there are no results yet) and explains the mechanism honestly. EMAIL 3 (first_results milestone) renders ONLY the ``results_summary`` the Owner typed (their real words, HTML-escaped); the schema REJECTS an empty summary for kind='first_results' (a results email can never be sent with no real data). Every nurture mail carries a GDPR unsubscribe link, and the broadcast EXCLUDES unsubscribed rows (a withdrawn consent never receives a nurture mail). The broadcast is owner-gated and CONFIRM-gated: confirm=false is a dry-run that counts the audience and sends nothing (no one-click mass-send); confirm=true sends best-effort per recipient (one bad address is 'deferred', never fails the batch, never 5xxs). The LINGUISTIC/result claims are the Owner's real words -- the system never invents a number to make the email more compelling.
rules.evidence: 2026-06-21 Sprint Waitlist-Nurture -- the waitlist captured emails and sent the welcome ("estás en la lista") on join, but had no nurture sequence: the silence post-signup kills waitlist->operator conversion. The anchor found Build A (welcome on join) + the admin view already live (5th sprint running with pre-existing core); the genuinely-new work was the EMAIL 2/EMAIL 3 milestone broadcast, built fully-wired (no dead button -- Reality Contract) behind the Owner's manual confirm trigger. Pairs with HR-DEMO-NO-FAKE-DATA, HR-WAITLIST-GATE, HR-6, the prompt-defense baseline (untrusted public name is HTML-escaped).
rules.exception: none -- promising a seat/timeline/revenue, or sending a 'results' email with a fabricated or absent figure, is forbidden. An honest "aún no hay plaza, te avisaremos" protects the relationship; a fabricated "tu plaza está lista en 48h" destroys it the moment it is not true.

enviar cualquier email de comunicacion comercial / marketing / nurture

rules.stop:
STOP si el email no lleva un footer de baja claro y funcional. Todo
