rules.title: the operator launch-readiness checklist shows the operator's REAL state; a step is `completed` ONLY when a concrete DB query proves it, and an unreadable/absent signal degrades to False (never a fabricated "done"); a step marked done that is not done gives a false sense of readiness, and when the first test then fails on something the checklist called finished, the operator's trust in the system collapses
rules.trigger: any code in engines/system/launch_readiness (get_launch_readiness / the 9 step signals / overall_status), api/launch_router (GET /api/v1/launch/readiness), the /app/launch page + LaunchReadinessChecklist, the Sidebar launch-link visibility, or scripts/update_system_health.py.
rules.stop: every step's `completed` is a real DB read, fail-open to False (an unreadable signal is "not done yet", never a fabricated "done"). Each of the 9 steps keys ONLY on a signal that actually populates in the operator flow: ACCOUNT_COMPLETE (authenticated email), MARKET_CONFIGURED (FirstWinProfile row), NICHE_SELECTED (GuidedStartProgress.chosen_niche), HOOK_CHOSEN (KIND_TEST_PLAN), BUDGET_SET (starting_capital>0), STORE_GENERATED (KIND_STORE_LIVE), STORE_APPROVED (KIND_STORE_LIVE APPROVED or ShopifyCredential active), META_CONNECTED (meta/tiktok ApiCredentialRecord), FIRST_TEST_LIVE (AdExecution RUNNING). KIND_CREATIVE is DELIBERATELY UNUSED (no production creation site -> keying a step on it would make READY permanently unreachable). TWO tenant keys are real (G2 HIGH-1): email-keyed tables (FirstWinProfile, GuidedStartProgress) vs brand-keyed tables (published store + ad credentials, written under resolve_operator_brand = op_xxxx for a provisioned subscriber) -- the brand-keyed queries MUST match either key (email, brand) or READY is unreachable for a paid subscriber. READY iff steps 1-8 (required) complete; FIRST_TEST_LIVE is the goal, not required. action_links are real /app routes (never a 404). update_system_health.py FAILS OPEN (missing env / network / non-200 / non-dict body -> preserve the prior doc, exit 1, never overwrite with a partial) + HR-4 atomic os.replace + carries the HR-INVESTOR-METRICS-HONEST markers, never a fabricated euro. The Sidebar launch link is fail-visible (hidden only when overall_status==READY; any error keeps it shown). Zero-DDL.
rules.evidence: 2026-06-21 SL-144 -- the gap was the first-cycle: a new operator had no screen telling them the path to their first test, and SYSTEM_HEALTH.md was a one-time static snapshot. G2 caught HIGH-1 (the email-vs-brand tenant-key mismatch made READY unreachable for the exact target persona) -- fixed by resolving the brand in the router and matching either key, with a regression test crossing the resolve_operator_brand seam. Pairs with HR-INVESTOR-METRICS-HONEST, HR-NBGAE-ONE-ACTION, HR-D14, HR-4.
rules.exception: none -- marking a step completed without a real DB verification, keying a step on a signal with no creation site, querying a brand-keyed table by email alone, or an action_link to a non-existent route is forbidden. A false "ready" costs the operator a failed first test and their trust.

rules.trigger:
escribir un post de blog dirigido a una persona/audiencia especifica
  (el que ya fallo su primer intento, el profesional con empleo fijo, el
  emprendedor latinoamericano, etc.).
rules.stop:
STOP si el copy intenta MOTIVAR en lugar de explicar el MECANISMO. Un
  post para una persona concreta reconoce su realidad sin exagerar ni minimizar.
  El que ya fallo NO necesita "esta vez sera diferente" — necesita entender
  exactamente que estuvo mal (el numero: CPA sin umbral de corte) y que mecanismo
  lo previene (el read a 7 dias frente al benchmark). El copy que respeta la
  inteligencia del lector convierte mejor que el que intenta motivarlo; la
  motivacion vacante (una conviccion sin mecanismo que la respalde) es peor que
  el silencio. Complementa HR-COPY-PRECISION-OVER-ENTHUSIASM y el COPY_STANDARD:
  mecanismo sobre promesa, ningun numero sin fuente, CW Ops comprime inteligencia
  y JAMAS dice que ejecuta los anuncios del operador, CTA = acceso (/#waitlist),
  Alvaro = primer y unico operador (nunca se implica un segundo).
rules.evidence:
2026-06-21, batch 4 (15 posts persona-specific). Cuatro gaps de 0
  cobertura confirmados por grep antes de escribir: el-que-fallo, el-profesional,
  LATAM-por-pais, Meta-avanzado. Se SELLA para que ningun post persona-specific
  caiga en la motivacion vacante — el modo de fallo natural cuando el lector trae
  una herida (fallo previo) o un miedo (arriesgar el sueldo): la tentacion de
  consolar en vez de explicar. El mecanismo es el respeto; la motivacion sin
  mecanismo es la falta de respeto.
rules.exception:
"owner: motiva sin mecanismo en este post" (literal, un solo post;
  jamas el default).

rules.trigger: surface operator-facing guidance during the first 7 days of an operator's first live test (a first-cycle view, a day-by-day guide, a "what should I do now" screen over a running experiment).
rules.stop: STOP if the guidance pushes the operator toward acting impulsively on thin data. During the evaluation window (days 1-6) the system EDUCATES the operator to NOT over-react: a CPA of EUR22 on day 3 does not mean the ad failed -- it means there are more days of data to collect before anyone can know. The "what NOT to do today" guidance is as important as the "what to do today" guidance, and is mandatory: the single most destructive first-cycle mistake is panic-killing a test on day 3, which destroys the very data the ECE needs to decide on day 7. A metric is ALWAYS shown with its phase so a number is never read out of context; the day-7 preview cites the REAL thresholds (SafetyConfig: auto-kill at CPA > kill_cpa_multiplier x the niche target or ROAS below kill_roas_floor; winner at ROAS >= scale_roas_target), never a fabricated per-niche euro. The day-7 milestone references the REAL observed result (SCALE/CONTINUE/INSUFFICIENT) and fires ONCE per operator's first cycle, only when the first test survived the auto-kill to day 7 (a test killed earlier already produced ad_killed -- no duplicate milestone). The phase view replaces the readiness checklist ONLY when a test is active (cycle_day != null); with no active test the checklist stays (SL-144). Observe-only, Zero-DDL: it reads the live SRE/RBD/NBGAE + ad_executions and educates, never spends/scales/pauses.
rules.evidence: 2026-06-21 SL-145 -- after SL-144 got the operator to READY and launched, a new gap opened: Alvaro opens the system on day 3, sees a CPA with no idea if it is good or bad, and the natural move (pause it) is the one move that destroys the cycle. AdExecution is email-keyed in the real launch flow (fwe_router passes _tenant=email), so the RAE engines and this evaluator key on email; the SL-144 brand-seam both-keys query is kept defensively. Pairs with HR-LAUNCH-CHECKLIST-REAL, HR-NBGAE-ONE-ACTION, HR-SRE-OBSERVE-NOT-ACT, HR-RBD-DIAGNOSIS-HONEST, UT-KARMA-SPECIFIC-MESSAGES, HR-4.
rules.exception: none -- showing a raw metric without its phase context, omitting the "what NOT to do" guidance, fabricating a per-niche euro threshold, or emitting the day-7 milestone more than once per first cycle is forbidden. A first-cycle number without context teaches the operator to panic-kill.

rules.trigger:
editar o crear el bloque de respuesta directa de un post (el campo
  `fast_answer` que se renderiza como aside "Respuesta rapida"/"Fast answer" al
  inicio, mas la seccion `## Fast answer` del cuerpo) con intencion AEO — hacer
  el post citable por ChatGPT / Perplexity / Claude AI / Google AI Overviews.
rules.stop:
STOP si el bloque introduce un numero que NO esta ya en el cuerpo del
  post con su fuente ya citada. El bloque de respuesta directa SOLO extrae al
  inicio el numero mas importante que el post ya prueba — nunca anade una cifra
  nueva para parecer "mas citable". Un numero inventado o sin fuente en la
  respuesta rapida contamina exactamente el contenido que sera citado por
  millones de personas via motores de IA; la precision importa mas aqui que en
  cualquier otro punto del post. Corolario operativo: si un post ya tiene un
  `fast_answer` que lidera con un numero con fuente (lo normal bajo el
  COPY_STANDARD), NO se anade un segundo bloque "respuesta rapida" — eso es un
  duplicado, no una mejora.
rules.evidence:
2026-06-22, sprint de internal-linking + AEO. El prompt pidio "anadir un
  bloque Respuesta rapida a 15 posts"; el anchor probo que el bloque YA existe
  site-wide (el aside de `fast_answer`) y que los 15 posts de mayor potencial AEO
  YA lideran con un numero con fuente (vault priors, Triple Whale 2025). Se SELLA
  para fijar que el bloque AEO es un EXTRACTO de lo ya probado, jamas una fuente
  de cifras nuevas, y que no se duplica un bloque ya presente. Complementa
  HR-COPY-PRECISION-OVER-ENTHUSIASM y el COPY_STANDARD.
rules.exception:
"owner: cita esta cifra sin fuente en el post, la respaldo yo"
  (literal, un solo post; jamas el default).

rules.trigger: surface a post-cycle debrief / "ciclo completado" view, a recommended next step after a cycle result, or any claim about how a completed cycle moved the CIS / system intelligence.
rules.stop: STOP if the debrief softens what actually happened or fabricates a CIS rise. The debrief reflects reality: a ROAS of 0.8x (below the safety threshold) is reported as "0.8x, por debajo del umbral" -- never "0.8x pero hay potencial" nor "con mas tiempo podria haber funcionado". Artificial euphoria makes the operator repeat the same mistake; the operator who understands exactly why it failed makes a better cycle-2 decision. Every euro/CPA/ROAS is a REAL value from the outcome row or omitted -- never invented (a None field is dropped, not fabricated). The CIS contribution is HONEST: a SINGLE operator's completed cycle does NOT raise the CIS -- data_sufficiency needs 2 INDEPENDENT operators to reach LIMITED and 3+ for HIGH (learning_velocity); the debrief says the cycle CONTRIBUTED a data point and the score rises on cross-operator confirmation, NEVER "el CIS subio de 3.0 a X" for one operator (HR-INVESTOR-METRICS-HONEST). The CIS itself is derived-live (recomputed on every /app/admin/intelligence read) -- there is no cache to invalidate; the only non-live surface is the static SYSTEM_HEALTH.md pitch doc, refreshed fail-open on cycle complete (preserve the prior doc on any error). The recommended next step ROUTES to the gate and NEVER spends: SCALE -> /app/campanas (the operator confirms the budget on the live winner), KILL/INSUFFICIENT -> Guided Start with the niche pre-filled (cycle 2); capital moves only at the operator's approval inside the gate.
rules.evidence: 2026-06-22 SL-145b -- after SL-145 taught Alvaro to wait until day 7, the gap was the day AFTER the result: the operator who does not know what to do after the first KILL never launches cycle 2, and no cycle 2 means no data and the CIS never rises. Two premises corrected at plan time: (1) the CIS is already derived-live -- nothing to recalculate; (2) a single operator's WINNER does NOT raise data_sufficiency (needs 2 independent operators), so claiming a rise would be dishonest. Pairs with HR-FIRST-CYCLE-PATIENCE, HR-INVESTOR-METRICS-HONEST, HR-NBGAE-ONE-ACTION, HR-LAUNCH-CHECKLIST-REAL, HR-4.
rules.exception: none -- softening a real failure, fabricating a euro/CPA/ROAS, claiming a single-operator CIS rise, or a next-step button that commits capital without the Guided Start / campanas gate is forbidden. A debrief that flatters the operator costs them their next cycle's capital and trust.

rules.trigger: surface the operator's own per-period P&L / financial history (revenue, ad spend, the 20% fee, net), a lifetime billing total, or any proactive "fee calculated / receipt" notice.
rules.stop: STOP if any figure is fabricated or the fee is presented opaquely. The operator has the right to audit exactly what they generated, what they paid in ads, what CW Ops charged as the fee, and what stayed in their Iron Vault -- a P&L is an OBLIGATION of a system that takes a 20% fee, not a favor; the transparency of the fee builds trust and its opacity destroys it even when the math is correct. The fee shown IS the D08 settlement (20% over VERIFIED matured profit minus the refund reserve -- HR-BILLING-MATURITY), NEVER a naive 20%x(gross-ad_spend): a just-closed month still in the refund window reads "pendiente" with fee EUR0, and a USD-spend / EUR-gross month with no Owner FX rate reads "divisas" and bills nothing (HR-CURRENCY-HONEST). net / roas / gross render as "no computable" (an em-dash), NEVER a fabricated EUR0, whenever they cannot be computed truthfully (currency mismatch, unmeasured gross -- UT-58: None != 0); the lifetime total flags when a month's net could not be included so it is never silently partial. The unit is the billing MONTH (period YYYY-MM), never a fabricated "per-cycle" P&L the billing system does not compute. A fee_receipt is emitted ONCE per matured (verified) month, deduped on the period -- never on a pending / mismatch month, never twice. The history NEVER re-derives the fee: it consumes the exact share_amount the D08 settlement already computed.
rules.evidence: 2026-06-22 SL revenue-history -- the prompt asked for a "per-cycle P&L" assuming the system "knows" gross/ad_spend/fee/net and a flat 20% fee. The anchor proved (1) the single-period D08 P&L already exists LIVE (SL-104 A, /app/configuracion Payments: gross/ad_spend/fee with a 3-month history); (2) the fee is NOT 20%x(gross-ads) but 20% over MATURED profit minus the refund reserve (D08), EUR0 while a month is in the refund window or currency-mismatched; (3) billing is per calendar MONTH, not per ad-test "cycle". Built only the genuine gaps -- a multi-period history endpoint + lifetime totals + a /app/capital panel + a once-per-matured-period fee_receipt -- reusing the live settlement (zero fee-logic duplication) and mirroring D08 exactly. Pairs with HR-BILLING-MATURITY, HR-CURRENCY-HONEST, HR-INVESTOR-METRICS-HONEST, HR-CYCLE-DEBRIEF-HONEST.
rules.exception:
none -- a fabricated revenue/fee/net figure, a fee shown as a naive 20%x(gross-ads) instead of the D08 settled value, a EUR0 where the truth is "unknown", or a per-cycle P&L the billing model does not produce is forbidden. An operator who cannot audit their own accounts cannot trust the system that manages their capital.
EXCEPCION literal del Owner: "owner: muestra esta cifra de P&L sin fuente DB" (un solo turno; jamas el default).

rules.trigger: a smoke / integration test fails because the LIVE behavior legitimately changed (a route now redirects, content moved, a topology inverted) -- NOT because of a code regression in the current sprint.
rules.stop: STOP and decide honestly. A smoke test that fails because reality changed is not a regression -- it is an OUTDATED test. Disabling or ignoring it is WORSE than updating it: a disabled test protects nothing. Update the assertion to verify the CORRECT behavior of the LIVE topology -- a check that now asserts a 308 redirect, an /en/* route, or a reworded hero is as valid as one asserting a 200. VERIFY the live behavior FIRST (probe the real route) before rewriting the assertion -- never change a check to assert a behavior the route does not actually exhibit. Never DELETE a check to make the suite green; UPDATE it. Updated checks are quality infrastructure, not technical bureaucracy.
rules.evidence: 2026-06-23, i18n-inversion stale-check sprint. The i18n topology inverted (/ = ES canonical, /en/* = EN) and deployed (589d289 / cbe09ef), but 16 smoke checks still asserted the pre-inversion topology (/ = EN, /es/* = ES) -> 16 false fails carried across 2+ sprints, eroding the smoke's signal (a chronically-red suite trains the team to ignore it). Each of the 16 was live-verified against prod before updating (EN routes -> /en/*, ES /es/* -> root slugs, /sitemap.xml ES-canonical, /legal = ES with toggle -> /en/legal, the LandingV2 reworded hero/CTA, moved inversor/precios source paths); 0 deleted; 453/453 green. Pairs with HR-MIGRATION-LIVE-SMOKE-IS-TRUTH, HR-REDIRECT-PERMANENTE, HR-I18N-ONE-LANDING-PER-LOCALE.
rules.exception: none -- and the inverse is equally binding: if the live behavior is WRONG (a sitemap genuinely 404s where it should serve, a link genuinely broken), the failing check found a REAL bug -> fix the bug, do NOT update the check to accept the breakage. The line is: update the check when reality is correct-but-changed; fix the code when reality is wrong.

rules.title: Classifier Blocks Claudesettingsjson Commands In
rules.trigger: Before writing any file under ~/.claude/ or any agent-owned global config
rules.stop: Ship the PP-internal half (hook script, command body); document the Owner-side registration step in the agent body. Do not advisory-tag the gap; document honestly per L no-classified-FAILs.
rules.evidence: [never_again] Classifier blocks ~/.claude/settings.json + commands/ in auto-mode

rules.title: Stalled/unbounded session is advisory only; never auto-kill
rules.trigger: The Process Hibernation governor (modules/cognitive_os/process_governor.py loop_advisory) flags a pane STALLED (no output >30min AND session >1h total) or UNBOUNDED (active >2h without a /compact reset).
rules.stop: Surface a VISIBLE advisory to the Owner ONLY. NEVER change the hibernate/keep verdict and NEVER auto-kill a process -- a long-running pane may be real continuous work; the Owner decides /kclear or close. Fail-open: unmeasurable session age -> silence (never a false positive). EXCEPCION: none -- no phrase authorizes auto-killing on a stall/unbounded signal.
rules.evidence: [kickbacks] a session hung ~10h undetected caused the Kickbacks suspension (2026-07-04); loop-boundedness added to the governor as a VISIBILITY net, never an autonomous kill. Sealed SCS C75; UKDL T-UNBOUNDED-SESSION-001.

rules.title: The pane_map is the only post-crash recovery mechanism; freshness is a production requirement
rules.trigger: Relying on ~/.claude/state/pane_map.md/.json for pane recovery, OR classifying a pane as LIVE.
rules.stop: (1) Freshness: the pane_map MUST be regenerated on a bounded cadence (PP-PaneMapUpdate scheduled task, every 5 min + onlogon). If it is stale > 5 min during active sessions the recovery net has already failed -- regenerate before trusting it. (2) Liveness: NEVER classify LIVE by file mtime -- a batch sweep (heartbeat merger, backup, git checkout, AV) rewrites transcript mtimes en masse and forges false-LIVE. Classify by the transcript's INTERNAL last-message timestamp OR the snapshot live-registry only. EXCEPCION: none.
rules.evidence: [rca 2026-07-06] Cursor crash -> pane_map 8 days stale (no scheduled task registered, only the Cursor extension regenerated it). Regen showed 30 "LIVE"; internal-timestamp forensics proved only 1 genuinely live (this session) + 35 files sharing one mtime spike ~11 min old while their real last turn was 14h-7d old. Fix: build_pane_map.ps1 Get-LastInternalAgeMin + PP-PaneMapUpdate task. UKDL T-PANE-MAP-STALENESS-ROOT-CAUSE-001 + T-PANE-MAP-FALSE-LIVE-MTIME-001. Sealed SCS C79.

rules.title: every economic field in niche_benchmarks.yaml has an EXTERNALLY-DOCUMENTED source (or the internal corpus) or is null; a fabricated CPA/CPM/AOV/ROAS/CR contaminates the PKM priors of EVERY operator in that niche, so the system prefers an honest null to an unbacked number

the FGR emergent notice tells the Owner the market produced a failure type the system had never seen; it is an OBSERVATION, not an alarm; OWNER-only; emitted ONCE per (niche, hook_type, failure_mode) at actionable (>= 2 independent operators) confidence -- never per observation, never for a single operator's bad luck
rules.trigger:
a failure type appears that was not in the priors. emit_emergent_pattern_notices (engines/cbin/failure_genome_registry) fires fgr_emergent_pattern from the outcome recorder after a LOSER/KILLED, fail-open.

RECOGNISE: (1) emitting on a single operator's emergent failure -- one operator's bad luck is not a market signal; the notice gates on ACTIONABLE (MEDIUM+, >= 2 independent operators), the FGR's own confidence doctrine. (2) re-emitting on every new observation -- the notice fires ONCE per (niche, hook_type, failure_mode); the durable dedup IS the notice log itself (payloads_of_type), further observations are consolidation not news (the STOP). The failure_mode is part of the dedup key (the same archetype can produce different new failure modes, each its own discovery). (3) surfacing it in an operator's feed -- it is persisted under CBIN_SYSTEM_TENANT, off the per-tenant Karma bus, never a per-operator autonomy toggle. (4) leaking identity -- the payload carries niche + hook archetype + failure_mode + counts, never a tenant_email (HR-D14). (5) breaking the kill -- the recorder hook is fail-open; a notice failure never blocks the kill that recorded the outcome.
rules.evidence: 2026-06-21 -- the FGR detected emergent patterns (SL-123) but no notification reached the Owner; the event was modelled on the CBIN milestone (SL-142, EVENT_CBIN_PATTERN_PROMOTED): system-sentinel tenant + log-based once-per-pattern dedup. Detect: test_fgr_emergent_event (V-FGR-EMERGENT-ONCE / -LOW / -KNOWN / -PER-PATTERN / -SEAM) + smoke [388]-[389]. Pairs with HR-FGR-EMERGENT-DETECTION, HR-CBIN-MILESTONE-HONEST, HR-D14, D14, D18.
rules.exception: the Owner may raise the act-on threshold (HIGH-only) or widen it to LOW; the dedup-once and owner-only and anonymized invariants are doctrine.

rules.title: the CBIN recommendation is a SUGGESTION based on the system's data, never a constraint; it explains WHY it recommends a hook (N HIGH patterns, N confirmations) but never blocks the operator from choosing a different one; confidence reaches HIGH ONLY through a 3+-independent-operator source
rules.trigger:
build_cbin_recommendation (engines/cbin/recommendation) synthesizes WPR+FGR+HGR+CDR+ODR+MSR+CDM+KC for a (niche, market), surfaced by GET /api/v1/cbin/recommend[/{niche}] + the /app/capital BestBetPanel + the Guided Start CbinRecommendationBanner.

RECOGNISE: (1) blocking other hooks -- the surfaces are RECOMMEND-ONLY; the Guided Start banner is a suggestion the operator may ignore (it pre-fills, never forces), and it is shown ONLY at confidence MEDIUM+ (nothing fabricated/low-evidence in the flow). (2) a HIGH confidence without 3+ operators -- HIGH is reachable ONLY via a WPR HIGH or KC compound HIGH source, each 3+ independent operators BY CONSTRUCTION (HR-WPR-CONFIDENCE-HONEST); a 2-operator WPR MEDIUM can reach MEDIUM, never HIGH (the STOP). (3) recommending a hook the FGR confirmed as a HIGH failure -- a FGR-HIGH archetype is removed from recommended and surfaced in avoid (never recommend an angle the system watched die). (4) a fabricated recommendation when empty -- an honest INSUFFICIENT with the early-operator message, never an invented hook. (5) scoping content per operator -- the recommendation is niche-level + cross-operator; the caller's identity resolves their market (source_market) ONLY, never the answer (HR-D14: hook archetypes / counts / price BANDS / density LEVEL, never an identity). (6) reaching the browser at bare /cbin -- nginx forwards only /api/, so the operator endpoint MUST be mounted under /api/v1 (the bare-/cbin registry router is smoke-only).
rules.evidence: 2026-06-21 -- the 6 CBIN registries were each queryable but the operator had to synthesize "what to do next" by hand; the PKM already did this fold internally for the ECE's pre-spend gate, so the synthesis was exposed directly to the operator. Detect: test_cbin_recommendation (V-RECO-FGR-EXCLUDE / -INSUFFICIENT / -NO-HIGH-WITHOUT-3OPS / -HIGH-3OPS / -ORDER / -MEDIUM-SIGNALS / -WIRING) + test_cbin_recommend_endpoint (V-RECO-EP-*) + smoke [394]-[397]. Pairs with HR-WPR-CONFIDENCE-HONEST, HR-FGR-EMERGENT-DETECTION, HR-CDR-CO-OCCURRENCE-HONEST, HR-D14.
rules.exception: the Owner may tune the confidence floor at which the Guided Start banner appears (MEDIUM+ default); the recommend-only, no-HIGH-without-3-operators, FGR-exclusion, honest-INSUFFICIENT and anonymized invariants are doctrine.

rules.title: **TRIGGER:** authoring or editing any page/post `title` that becomes the `<title>` tag (blog frontmatter, route `generateMetadata`, any indexable surface).
rules.stop: ** STOP if the rendered `<title>` exceeds **60 chars** (target band **50–60**, hard floor **40**). Google truncates >60 chars in the SERP — the user sees `...` instead of the CTA or the secondary keyword, and CTR drops because the title cannot finish its job. 50–60 is functional, not cosmetic: it is the range where Google shows the full title on most devices. A keyword well-placed in a 55-char title beats the same keyword in a 90-char title that gets cut. When trimming: drop the least-informative trailing words first, never the front (intent) keyword; cut after a `:`/`—` separator when what follows adds nothing; the trimmed title must keep the SAME meaning or a more concentrated one — never less. Verify the RENDERED `<title>` length (account for any `title.template` brand suffix in a layout), not just the source string.
rules.evidence: ** TUA-X blog audit 2026-06-22 found 155/214 served titles >60 chars (avg 68.7, max 120; only 44 in the 50–60 band) emitted verbatim as `<title>` — systemic SERP-truncation/CTR loss across the long tail. Trimmed all 155 to 40–60 (161 in band, 0 over 60, 0 under 40), front keyword preserved, Zero-DDL, live 4b5d649.
rules.exception: ** Owner says literally "title length exempt this turn".

rules.title: **TRIGGER:** publishing dated/auto-generated/high-similarity blog content (weekly digests, programmatic series, anything where week-N and week-N+1 are ~the same shape over different ephemeral data).
rules.stop: ** keep it `noindex` (served + crawlable, OUT of the index/sitemap/grid), NOT deleted and NOT `published:false`. noindex does NOT mean the content is bad — it means dated content with high week-to-week similarity can CANNIBALISE the evergreen posts of the same cluster: Google cannot tell which week-X is the most relevant result for "fitness ad intelligence", so it splits/confuses the ranking signal. noindex removes that confusion — the digests stay a resource for operators who seek them directly (direct URL / internal link), but they stop competing with the evergreen pillars. Wire noindex as a REAL mechanism distinct from `published:false`: the post must still be SERVED (200, generateStaticParams keeps it) + emit `robots:{index:false,follow:true}` + be filtered from the sitemap, the listing grid, and related-post pools. A frontmatter `noindex:true` with no code wiring is a no-op (Mistake #16 — compiles != works).
rules.evidence: ** TUA-X blog 2026-06-25 (SL-blog-noindex-weekly-digests). 20 weekly-intelligence digests (2026-w22..w26 fitness/skincare, EN+ES) were dated thin content overlapping the evergreen meta-ads pillars. The prompt assumed `noindex:true` would auto-drop them from the sitemap "like the templates" — but templates use `published:false` (which 404s); noindex was NOT wired for blog posts. Wired BlogFrontmatter.noindex + listIndexablePosts() for grid/sitemap, kept listBlogPosts for routing (200), robots noindex in both [slug] routes. Live f4e8788: sitemaps EN 104->94 / ES 112->102, digests 200+noindex, indexable posts intact. Zero-DDL, tsc 0.
rules.exception: ** Owner says literally "index the digests this turn".

rules.title: **TRIGGER:** making the landing / public surface "look rigorous to an LLM or operator" — repositioning generic AI-marketing claims into specific, sourced ones; adding trust/entity schema (Organization knowsAbout/sameAs, Article citation); or any claim about what the system "learns" / "knows" / "does".
rules.stop: ** make VISIBLE what is already TRUE, never manufacture rigor. Four sub-rules: (1) **Real mechanism, not the prompt's example.** A prompt may suggest a vivid claim ("corta si el CPA supera 2× el threshold en 7 días") that the system does NOT actually implement — verify against the engine/the data posts before asserting. The TUA-X auto-kill is two SOURCED thresholds read together (CPA vs niche band, CTR vs floor) over a window that DEPENDS ON CONVERSION VOLUME, explicitly NOT a fixed day count; asserting "7 días" would contradict the system's own honest explanation. (2) **No fabricated entity links.** `sameAs` means "same entity, other verified profile" — if no verified social account exists (site-metadata forbids it, Apex Rule 9), use the REAL relationship (`parentOrganization`/`knowsAbout`/`slogan`) instead of inventing a profile URL. (3) **Citations only where the source is named in-prose.** Opt-in `sources` frontmatter → Article `citation` emitted ONLY for posts that actually cite the source (Triple Whale 2025, internal vault bands); absent → no citation key, never a fabricated source. (4) **Honesty IS the credibility signal under the 1-operator constraint.** With one live operator, describe the network rule as a RULE ("3+ operators → HIGH; 1 → INSUFFICIENT, the system does not act") and the state as INSUFFICIENT — never imply an achieved network effect. STOP if a new claim is more promissory than the system can demonstrate today.
rules.evidence: ** TUA-X landing GEO/trust sprint 2026-06-25 (SL-landing-geo-trust, branch sprint/landing-geo-trust off main 7036769, FF-ready). Anchor caught FOUR prompt premises false: (a) prod was not 4f283ad; (b) the landing→blog bridge already existed (ProofStrip latest-3) → refined to curated data posts, not rebuilt; (c) blog posts ALREADY had full Article+FAQPage schema (postStructuredData, @type:"Article" not "BlogPosting") → Build C reduced to the genuinely-absent knowsAbout/slogan/parentOrganization + opt-in citation, honoring "STOP si Build C duplica schema existente"; (d) the suggested 2×CPA/7-day kill mechanism was fabricated vs the real volume-dependent two-threshold rule. Also caught: a sibling (fix/cw-landing-honesto-bridge-es, unmerged, stale STEPS-array) owns the hero/step-03 copy → Build A confined to step-01 + ProofStrip (zero collision). Activated a dead vitest (blogSchema.test.ts → .tsx; config include was .tsx-only since Sprint 40). **DEDUP OUTCOME (2026-06-26, prod 88ad051):** before deploy, re-anchoring found TWO MORE panes had already shipped ~75% of this sprint to main — seo/blog-hreflang-reciprocal (d451e38) shipped the ComoFunciona claims + the identical ProofStrip curation; seo/geo-trust-schema merged Org knowsAbout + Article citation (more completely: 15 mdx + BlogPostView). Owner chose HowTo-only; dropped Build A/B/C; shipped ONLY the genuinely-new HowTo node (live fe96bca, +97/0-del, smoke 14/14). A first HowTo commit nearly regressed prod — a worktree file-sync daemon + advancing main made a pathspec commit DELETE the sibling's live knowsAbout; caught by `git diff kobicraft/main HEAD` showing `-knowsAbout` and fixed by re-cherry-picking the additive Build D and verifying 0 deletions in the COMMITTED OBJECT before push. Net lesson: in an N-pane repo, re-anchor every sub-build against current main + sibling branches immediately before deploy, ship only the proven-unique delta, and verify the committed-object diff (not worktree/index) is additive.
rules.exception: ** Owner says literally "make the claim aspirational this turn".

rules.title: **TRIGGER:** any code path that launches a headless browser / Playwright / Chromium for a screenshot, visual-regression, or DOM-render check (`playwright`, `chrome --headless`, `--screenshot`, puppeteer, any pixel-diff harness).
rules.stop: ** the browser runs on the VPS (204.168.166.63, host `ubuntu-8gb-hel1`) or GEX44 — NEVER on the Owner's laptop, where a headless browser eats local RAM. Enforce it IN CODE, not by memory: the harness's first executable line calls a VPS guard (`vps_guard.assert_vps()`) that aborts (exit 2) on a local/Windows/non-allowlisted host. A local-invoked runner must ship the harness over ssh to the VPS, run it there, and pull the report back — it must never fall through to launching a browser locally. The Owner should never have to remember the rule; a Playwright script WITHOUT the guard is itself the bug.
rules.evidence: ** TUA-X qa-system-vps-playwright sprint 2026-07-10 (SL-115). Built `scripts/qa/` (unified runner + VPS-only visual regression) to replace the manual HR-VISUAL eyeball on the operator dashboard calco. Two engineering traps surfaced and are folded into the harness: (1) a golden baseline must be a re-render of the SAME document, not preview-vs-reference-mockup — two different documents diverge ~6% on imperceptible dark-mode text-edge AA (Linux FreeType) while visually identical, which would make a 1% threshold unusable; same-document renders sit at 0.0000%. (2) the fixture must be render-normalized (fixed font kills the `fonts.googleapis.com` load race; frozen CSS animations stop a pulse ring screenshotting a random frame) — applied to the fixture only, prod CSS untouched. Proven end-to-end on the VPS: clean 0.0000% PASS (x2 deterministic), injected drift 55.56% FAIL, restored 0.0000% PASS. Pairs with HR-2 (green smoke before deploy) and HR-VISUAL-01/02 (pixel-exact calco).
rules.exception: ** Owner says literally "run playwright local this turn" (or sets `QA_FORCE_VPS=1` on a host they KNOW is an approved render box).
